name: Bank

strats.siteContext: 1F bomb site centered on Tellers' Office (long counter room) and Tellers curved area. Tellers' Office is accessible via floor destruction from 2F Banana or direct Lobby/Stairs approach. Primary vertical pressure comes from Banana floor destruction. Site rewards methodical Lobby control and careful counter-breach timing. Multiple vertical and ground-level approach options available.
strats.content:
# Bank — Tellers / Tellers' Office (1F) · ATTACK

**Status:** ⚠️ Partially Developed
**Formation:** 3-2
**Developed:** 2026-03-14
**Data Source:** STACK_03 migration
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej replaces Slug. Slug's "Hard Breach / Floor Destruction" role (soft breach) needs review — Peej (Ash/Brava) does not run hard breach. Assign breach to Sarge and use Peej for entry pressure on 1F rotations instead. See STACK_03_ATTACK_STRATS.md for the note on this strat.

> **Site Context:** 1F bomb site centered on Tellers' Office (long counter room) and Tellers curved area. Tellers' Office is accessible via floor destruction from 2F Banana or direct Lobby/Stairs approach. Primary vertical pressure comes from Banana floor destruction. Site rewards methodical Lobby control and careful counter-breach timing. Multiple vertical and ground-level approach options available.

---

## Spawn

3-man and 2-man groups spawn to control Lobby access and Banana stairwell simultaneously. Coordinate to establish both ground-level and vertical pressure on Tellers site.

---

## Roles

| Player | Role | Operator | Group |
|--------|------|----------|-------|
| **Hound** | Vertical / Vert | Flex | 3-man |
| **Sarge** | Vertical / Entry | Flex | 3-man |
| **Smigs** | Map Control / Support | Flex | 3-man |
| **Peej** | Hard Breach / Floor Destruction | Flex | 2-man |
| **Grant** | Anti-gadget / Flex | Flex | 2-man |

**Hound** — Upstairs through Square/Stock/Banana with Sarge and Smigs. Play vertical, take Banana map control. Destroy Banana floor (to 1F Tellers) or support floor destruction for vertical pressure. Denies roamer rotations.

**Sarge** — Upstairs through Square/Stock with Hound and Smigs. Play vertical, take map control. Coordinates Banana floor destruction timing. First through vertical entry once floor is open.

**Smigs** — Upstairs through Square/Stock with Sarge and Hound. Watch flanks, take map control. Utility support. Anti-gadget coverage on 2F approach.

**Peej** — Open area with Grant. Manage Lobby control. Breach Tellers' Office (prevent first floor rotations). Open small office wall into Tellers from Lobby side. Cuts off map.

**Grant** — Open area with Peej. Manage Lobby area. Prevent first floor rotations. Anti-gadget support on approach. Can open walls or entry fragger depending on comp.

---

## Execute Sequence

1. **Drone phase** — Grant/Peej drone Lobby, Tellers area, and Main Stairs to identify anchor positions. 3-man drones upper approach through Stock/Banana/Meeting areas. Identify roamer positions.

2. **Lobby control** — Peej/Grant establish control of 1F Lobby area. Deny early roamer rotations. Force defenders to commit to site early. Control ATM entrance and main entry points.

3. **Upper approach** — 3-man moves through 2F Stock/Meeting/Banana area. Take Banana hallway and establish floor destruction readiness.

4. **Banana floor destruction** — Destroy Banana floor (above 1F Tellers) to create vertical pressure and open entry point from 2F. Call destruction ready.

5. **Simultaneous ground + vertical** — 3-man drops into Tellers via Banana floor opening. Peej/Grant executes ground-level Lobby→Tellers' Office approach simultaneously.

6. **Counter approach** — Tellers is exposed once both pressure vectors commit. Defenders cannot hold both vertical (Banana) and ground (Lobby) simultaneously.

7. **Tellers take** — Establish dominance in Tellers area. Secure Tellers' Office counter position. Open destructible walls to expand space.

8. **Plant** — Plant in Tellers' Office counter area or back corner depending on anchor positions. Protect plant from Lobby roamer approach and vertical pressure from Banana above.

---

## Plant Position (PP)

**Primary:** Tellers' Office back/counter area — protected from main door entry and Banana vertical angle
**Alternate:** Tellers curved area if Tellers' Office counter too contested; less ideal but still defensible with vertical support

---

## Post-Plant

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Sarge** | Tellers' Office / Counter | Holds main plant angles from counter position |
| **Hound** | Banana / Above site | Maintains vertical control; prevents defender return from 2F |
| **Smigs** | Lobby-side door | Watches for Lobby roamer counter-push; denies ground-level re-entry |
| **Peej** | Main Stairs / Lobby | Protects backside entry points; cuts off perimeter rotations |
| **Grant** | ATM area / Entry control | Watches flanks; adapts to defender rotation attempts |

---

## Watch For

- **Banana roamer (strong setup)** — Defenders often position in Banana to contest floor destruction. Aggressive Banana clear is critical. 3-man should identify roamer position via drone before committing to floor destruction.

- **Lobby anchor (expected)** — Strong anchor may position in Lobby behind columns. Expect this. Peej/Grant should drone Lobby columns thoroughly before committing.

- **Aruni gate on Tellers' door** — Expected defensive gadget placement. Grant covers anti-gadget. If Aruni blocks Tellers' entry, use alternate approach or fall back to vertical-first strategy.

- **Bandit / Kaid on Banana floor destruction wall** — Expected electrical denial. Hound EMPs first. If wall is tricked with no EMP left, pivot to ground-only approach.

- **Mira Black Mirror on Tellers' windows** — May be placed to deny Lobby sightlines into Tellers. Breaching window destroys mirror. Call it immediately.

- **Lesion (Gu mines)** — Seeded in Lobby and Banana approach corridors. Ping tells Lesion the team position. Move with intent through approach areas.

- **Mute Signal Disruptors on Banana** — Can block utility placement or drone feeds. If Banana is heavily jammed, expect Mute — reposition approach or manual clear.

- **Tachanka (Shumikha grenades)** — Can create fire denial zones in Lobby or Tellers. If grenades block approach, wait for fire to fade or use vertical approach instead.

- **Tellers counter-anchor** — Defenders may position at Tellers counter facing Lobby. Expected setup. Drone counter position carefully before committing to ground entry.

---

## Win Conditions

There is more than one way to win this site. The execute sequence above is **Win A**. If conditions change mid-round, call the audible early — don't burn time forcing a denied path.

---

### Win A — Primary: Vertical Floor Destruction + Ground Lobby Pressure ✅
**When:** Banana floor accessible, Lobby is controllable, utility connects on approach.

3-man destroys Banana floor and drops vertical pressure. Peej/Grant executes simultaneous ground-level Lobby→Tellers pressure. Multiple angles overwhelm defenders. Plant in Tellers' Office.

**Who calls it:** Hound calls Banana floor destruction ready. Peej confirms Lobby clear and ground approach ready. Simultaneous commitment on both calls.

---

### Win B — Banana Floor Denied / Inaccessible ⚠️
**When:** Roamer controls Banana, floor destruction is blocked, or utility cannot place on Banana floor.

**Adjustment:**
- Abandon vertical approach entirely. Focus on ground-level Lobby→Tellers execute only.
- 3-man comes down stairs to assist 1F team (5v3 or 5v4 pressure on ground level).
- Shift to pure ground-level execution. Peej/Grant establish Lobby dominance.
- Use Lobby-to-Tellers' direct approach — single vector but still effective with numbers.
- Slower execute, but avoids denied vertical route.

**Who calls it:** Hound calls "Banana locked, going ground-only" after confirming floor destruction is not viable.

---

### Win C — Lobby Heavily Fortified ⚠️
**When:** Multiple anchors dig into Lobby with strong positioning; roamers prevent ground approach.

**Adjustment:**
- Defer ground approach. Go full **vertical-first strategy** — 3-man takes Banana without waiting for ground team.
- Drop Banana floor and pressure vertically. Drop directly into Tellers via Banana opening.
- Peej/Grant stay in Lobby/ATM area to deny roamer flank return but don't force a bad ground engagement.
- Once vertical entry is successful, collapse ground team into site after vertical secures space.
- Play for vertical advantage — ground team supports vertical team, not the reverse.

**Who calls it:** Sarge calls "Lobby locked, going vertical-first" after ground approach assessment fails.

---

### Win D — Early Man Advantage (Support Gets Pick)
**When:** Peej or Grant gets one or more kills during Lobby control phase, creating a 5v4 or 5v3.

**Adjustment:**
- **Accelerate execute immediately** — do not wait for full setup.
- If 1F flex gets kill: commit to ground Tellers execute NOW. 3-man still destroys Banana floor — still both pressure vectors.
- Use man-advantage to force plant faster. Defenders are thinner.
- Push aggressive tempo — play for quick plant.

**Who calls it:** Peej/Grant calls every kill. Full team pushes on man-advantage call immediately.

---

## Audibles

- **Banana blocked entirely / heavy roamer presence:** Full team goes ground-only 1F execute. 3-man comes down stairs.
- **Both 1F flex players (Peej/Grant) dead early:** 3-man must play slow and careful for ground-level pressure. Vertical pressure alone is not enough — need both vectors.
- **Lobby roamer gets multiple kills:** Fall back from Lobby approach. Let roamer overextend or regroup after resetting positions.
- **Utility fully denied / no EMPs left:** Play for picks and map control. Reset and try again. Don't force a denied wall with no utility.

strats.siteContext: 2F bomb site with two distinct rooms. CEO Office is vulnerable to Skylight hatch above and floor destruction from 1F Archives. Teller's Office features teller counter windows with views to 1F Tellers. Primary execute involves Skylight destruction, utility setup, and coordinated multi-angle pressure.
strats.content:
# Bank — CEO Office / Teller's Office (2F) · ATTACK

**Status:** ⚠️ Partially Developed
**Formation:** 3-1-1
**Developed:** 2026-03-14
**Data Source:** STACK_03 migration
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej replaces Slug. No hard-breach dependency in Peej's role here — name swap only. Peej's entry fragger identity (Ash/Brava) fits the solo flex role.

> **Site Context:** 2F bomb site with two distinct rooms. CEO Office is vulnerable to Skylight hatch above and floor destruction from 1F Archives. Teller's Office features teller counter windows with views to 1F Tellers. Primary execute involves Skylight destruction, utility setup, and coordinated multi-angle pressure.

---

## Spawn

3-man group spawns at the spawn closest to site (Boulevard or Alley side). Two solos each take one of the remaining two spawns, establishing multi-floor pressure coordination early.

---

## Roles

| Player | Role | Operator | Group |
|--------|------|----------|-------|
| **Hound** | Map Control / Support | Flex | 3-man |
| **Sarge** | Breach / Entry | Flex | 3-man |
| **Smigs** | Anti-gadget / Utility | Flex | 3-man |
| **Peej** | Flex / Backside | Flex | Solo |
| **Grant** | IQ / Flex / Cross angles | Flex | Solo |

**Hound** — Pushes Square/Stock with Sarge and Smigs. Takes Stock, watches Janitor and Hallway to cut off rotations. Supports primary breach utility setup.

**Sarge** — Pushes Square/Stock with Smigs and Hound. Hard breach support or entry fragger role. Likely plants once walls are open. First through primary breach.

**Smigs** — Pushes Square/Stock with Sarge and Hound. Supports breach, watches potential flanks with utility/suppressing fire. Anti-gadget coverage on approach.

**Peej** — Solo flex role. Plays by ear — likely backside from Lobby or below site. Cuts off rotations, isolated picks. Can cross from unexpected angles.

**Grant** — Solo flex/cross role. Takes site windows, gets cross through site. Protects plant once down. Provides secondary pressure vector.

---

## Execute Sequence

1. **Drone phase** — Grant drones the approach and site interior to identify anchor positions. 3-man drones top approach through Stock/Meeting areas. Peej drones potential flank routes.

2. **Utility setup** — 3-man establishes utility denial (EMPs, smokes, utility clear) on approach to Executive Lounge and Exec Hallway. Stack positions for simultaneous pressure.

3. **Stock clear** — Hound/Smigs/Sarge clear Stock Hallway/Stock Room area to prevent roamer flank during CEO push. Suppress any rotating defenders.

4. **Skylight destruction** — Destroy Skylight hatch above CEO early to deny vertical defender rotations and establish site control from above.

5. **CEO pressure begins** — 3-man moves into Executive Lounge/Exec Hallway area. Breach CEO door/wall with coordinated utility support. Sarge entry first.

6. **Parallel Teller's pressure** — Grant coordinates Teller's Office approach from below via floor destruction or Banana stairs. Creates simultaneous pressure vectors.

7. **Floor destruction** — Destroy CEO floor to 1F Archives and Banana floor to 1F Tellers once walls are breached. Deny defender cross angles.

8. **Plant** — Plant in CEO or Teller's depending on anchor positions. Protect plant from both bomb room angles and vertical pressure from below.

---

## Plant Position (PP)

**Primary:** CEO Office — behind desk or center cover (protected from Skylight angle once hatch is destroyed)
**Alternate:** Teller's Office — if anchors already cleared from CEO side; uses counter for plant protection

---

## Post-Plant

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Sarge** | CEO / Teller's site | Holds cross angles through site |
| **Hound** | Stock/Executive Lounge | Covers approaching angles; watches for roamer re-entry |
| **Smigs** | Hallway / Meeting area | Watches for rotation pressure from rest of 2F |
| **Peej** | Backside / Basement | Holds rotation path; denies flanking from unexpected vectors |
| **Grant** | Cross angle / Teller's side | Holds secondary cross; protects plant from Teller's angle if needed |

---

## Watch For

- **Skylight roamer** — One of the most common defender positions. Destroy Skylight early and stack upper position to deny re-entry. Identify roamer position via drone before committing to breach.

- **Aruni gate on CEO doorframe** — Smigs covers anti-gadget. If Aruni gate blocks primary breach, use secondary destruction route or fall back to soft wall alternative.

- **Bandit / Kaid electrical denial** — Expected on main walls. Hound EMPs first. If wall is tricked, call it and pivot to alternate angle. Do not waste utility on denied wall.

- **Mira Black Mirror** — On CEO or Teller's windows. Breaching opens the wall and destroys Mira simultaneously. Call it immediately so team knows mirror is gone and camera coverage changes.

- **Mute Signal Disruptors** — Can block utility placement or drone feeds in approach hallways. If drones get jammed early, suspect Mute — reposition drone entry or manual clear.

- **Fenrir (F-NATT gas)** — If present, expect canisters inside CEO near the plant spot. Pop them before planting — use grenade utility to detonate before approaching defuser.

- **Lesion (Gu mines)** — Seeded in approach paths and site interior. Ping tells Lesion your position. Move through approach with intent; don't sprint into known trap zones.

- **3-anchor defense** — Typical setup is one anchor in CEO (hard desk angle), one in Teller's (counter windows), one flexible in Executive Lounge or roaming. Drone pre-execute to identify positions.

---

## Win Conditions

There is more than one way to win this site. The execute sequence above is **Win A**. If conditions change mid-round, call the audible early — don't burn time trying to force a denied path.

---

### Win A — Primary: Coordinated Multi-Angle Pressure ✅
**When:** Utility connects on approach, Skylight destroyed early, primary walls accessible.

3-man opens CEO via Executive Lounge/Exec Hallway. Grant coordinates Teller's pressure from parallel angle. Simultaneous pressure overwhelms defender stacking. Plant in CEO.

**Who calls it:** Sarge calls the breach go once utility is confirmed connected. Grant confirms Teller's side clear before commitment.

---

### Win B — Skylight Inaccessible ⚠️
**When:** Skylight cannot be destroyed early due to defender positioning or utility denial.

**Adjustment:**
- Do not waste time trying to force Skylight destruction.
- Focus on ground-level CEO breach via Executive Lounge and Exec Hallway.
- Stack multiple angles on CEO door to overwhelm defenders on-site.
- Increase post-plant positioning to compensate for lack of vertical control.
- Teller's becomes secondary pressure — main force commits to CEO only.

**Who calls it:** Hound calls "Skylight denied, going ground-only" after first destruction attempt fails.

---

### Win C — Primary Walls Heavily Denied ⚠️
**When:** Bandit/Kaid fully deny both CEO approach routes with no EMP coverage remaining.

**Adjustment:**
- Abort the primary execute immediately — do not waste utility on denied walls.
- Pivot to **soft wall destruction alternative** if available in Executive Lounge or meeting areas.
- Consider Teller's as **primary objective** instead — may have clearer approach via Banana.
- Play for picks and numbers advantage reset. Do not force a failed breach.
- If both directions are shut: fall back to perimeter squeeze and attacker spawn control.

**Who calls it:** Sarge calls "walls denied, pivoting Teller's-first" or "abort execute, playing for picks."

---

### Win D — Early Man Advantage (Support Gets Pick)
**When:** Peej or Grant gets one or more kills during setup/approach phase, creating a 5v4 or 5v3.

**Adjustment:**
- This is a **gift round** — accelerate tempo.
- If advantage is confirmed, do not wait for full drone readiness. Push execute sooner.
- 3-man commits harder to CEO knowing site is thinner defensively.
- Grant/Peej press their advantage — don't let defenders reset their positions.

**Who calls it:** Peej/Grant calls every kill. Team pushes immediately on man-advantage call.

---

## Audibles

- **CEO locked / can't breach primary angle:** Fallback to Executive Lounge soft wall destruction or Teller's as primary objective.
- **Both breach players (Sarge/Smigs) dead early:** Go to pick-based round. Do not attempt execute with no breach utility.
- **Utility fully denied / no EMPs left:** Play for positions and picks, not the breach. Reset and attacker spawn control.
- **Roamer flanks during setup:** Abort current execute, regroup, and clear flank threat before re-committing.

strats.siteContext: Basement bomb site centered around Vault (single-entry heavily-reinforced room) and Server Room (large open room with pillars). Primary advantage is vertical pressure from above via multiple ceiling hatches. Coordinated vertical destruction and hatch stacking overwhelm basement defense. Multiple approach vectors available (stairs, basement corridors, vertical hatches).
strats.content:
# Bank — Vault / Server Room (Basement) · ATTACK

**Status:** ⚠️ Partially Developed
**Formation:** 3-2
**Developed:** 2026-03-14
**Data Source:** STACK_03 migration
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej replaces Slug. Vertical / Hatch role is operator-agnostic — name swap only. Peej's Ash/Brava works in this vertical pressure role.

> **Site Context:** Basement bomb site centered around Vault (single-entry heavily-reinforced room) and Server Room (large open room with pillars). Primary advantage is vertical pressure from above via multiple ceiling hatches. Coordinated vertical destruction and hatch stacking overwhelm basement defense. Multiple approach vectors available (stairs, basement corridors, vertical hatches).

---

## Spawn

3-man and 2-man groups spawn to enable stairwell control and vertical hatch positioning. Vertical coordination is the key — stack players to pressure multiple hatch points simultaneously (Open Space, Admin, Square, Elevators).

---

## Roles

| Player | Role | Operator | Group |
|--------|------|----------|-------|
| **Hound** | Shield / Push | Flex | 3-man |
| **Sarge** | Osa / Wall Opener | Flex | 3-man |
| **Smigs** | Hard Breach | Flex | 3-man |
| **Peej** | Vertical / Hatch | Flex | 2-man |
| **Grant** | Map Control / Frontline | Flex | 2-man |

**Hound** — Push through dirt/basement approach into Server with Sarge and Smigs. Take Server, help open walls, get plant down. Post-plant: watch opposite of Sarge for cross.

**Sarge** — Push through basement approach into Server with Hound and Smigs. Open walls, support breach, establish cross for plant assist. Likely plants.

**Smigs** — Push through basement approach with Hound and Sarge. Hard breach on Server walls, then watch flank during post-plant. Utility support on approach.

**Peej** — Vertical player on hatches upstairs. Destroys ceiling hatches from 1F Open Space, Admin, Square to open basement from above. Drop backside when 3-man execution begins on ground level.

**Grant** — Map Control / Frontline. Also plays hatches upstairs. Watches flanks, either drops backside or stays on hatches to cross from above. Coordinates vertical pressure timing.

---

## Execute Sequence

1. **Drone phase** — Grant/Peej drone basement approach (Lockers, Red Hallway, Vault Lobby) to identify anchor positions. 3-man drones 1F approach routes. Identify roamer positions before committing.

2. **1F clear** — 3-man methodically clears 1F approach areas (Square, Admin, Open Space) to establish upper-level control and deny roamer flanks during vertical setup.

3. **Hatch destruction begins** — Peej/Grant position upstairs on predetermined hatches (Open Space → Lockers, Admin → CCTV, Square → Server). Call timing for simultaneous hatch destruction.

4. **Simultaneous hatch drop** — Destroy multiple ceiling hatches at the same time (ideally 2–3 hatches in sequence). Overwhelming vertical pressure disorients defenders.

5. **Basement approach** — 3-man pushes through basement corridors (via Stairs/Lockers approach) into Server while vertical pressure is active. Smigs opens walls, Sarge/Hound establish Server control.

6. **Server taken** — Establish dominance in Server Room using pillar cover. Wall destruction enables expansion.

7. **Vault coordination** — If Vault is secondary objective, coordinate pressure from Vault Lobby side. Single entry makes it defendable — stack overwhelming numbers if going for Vault.

8. **Plant** — Plant in Server or Vault depending on anchor positions and vertical pressure success. Protect plant from all angles (stairs, basement corridors, hatches from above).

---

## Plant Position (PP)

**Primary:** Server Room — behind pillars or far corner (protected from basement corridor entries and hatch approaches)
**Alternate:** Vault Lobby or Lockers area if Server Room too contested; less ideal but still defensible

---

## Post-Plant

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Sarge** | Server / Plant protection | Holds main cross angles; watches for basement roamer counter-push |
| **Hound** | Opposite Sarge angle | Cross cover; prevents flanking from alternate entry |
| **Smigs** | Basement corridor / stairwell | Watches for rotation pressure from upper floors |
| **Peej** | Above (hatch position) | Continues vertical coverage; prevents defender return from 1F |
| **Grant** | Roam / Adapt | Watches secondary flanks, protects plant from unexpected angles |

---

## Watch For

- **Vault roamer (if applicable)** — Defenders may position in Vault Lobby or Vault Entrance early. Drone Intel critical before basement approach. If roamer commits early, pressure from stairwell immediately.

- **Red Hallway fortress setup** — Defenders may anchor Red Hallway with reinforcements blocking Lockers approach. Expected setup. 3-man should expect this and have secondary approach via Blue Stairs (1F Square route).

- **Jäger ADIS / Wamai Magnets** — May be placed on hatches to deny vertical destruction. If utility is airborne, drones will catch it. Call it and use alternate hatch or manual clear.

- **Bandit / Kaid on basement walls** — Expected on Vault entry or Server approach walls. Smigs covers anti-gadget — EMP first. If wall is tricked and no EMP left, pivot to alternate approach.

- **Lesion (Gu mines)** — Seeded in basement corridors and stairwell landings. Ping tells Lesion the 3-man position. Move through corridors with intent; don't sprint.

- **Mira Black Mirror** — On basement walls or vault entry. If destroyed by wall breach, mirror dies with it. Call it immediately so defenders know coverage is gone.

- **Capitão (fire grenades)** — Can block staircases or basement corridors with flame line denial. If fire blocks primary stairs, use alternate approach (Blue Stairs) or wait for fire to fade.

- **Multiple vertical defenders** — If defenders are already positioned at hatch landing zones before destruction, expect direct gunfight on hatch drops. Stack overwhelming numbers on each hatch.

---

## Win Conditions

There is more than one way to win this site. The execute sequence above is **Win A**. If conditions change mid-round, call the audible early — don't burn time forcing a denied path.

---

### Win A — Primary: Coordinated Vertical Stack + Ground Breach ✅
**When:** Multiple hatches destructible, basement approach accessible, vertical pressure connected.

Simultaneous hatch destruction and 3-man basement push overwhelm defenders. Multiple pressure vectors prevent stacking. Plant in Server Room.

**Who calls it:** Grant calls hatch timing from above. Sarge confirms basement approach go. Smigs detonates utility on signal.

---

### Win B — Vertical Hatches Denied ⚠️
**When:** Hatches are defended, roamers control landing zones, or utility cannot be placed on hatches.

**Adjustment:**
- Abandon vertical approach entirely. Focus on ground-level basement breach only.
- 3-man commits to stairwell control and basement corridor approach without hatch support.
- Increase stairwell utility (smokes, flashes) to compensate for lost vertical advantage.
- Stack heavier on ground entry — no aerial support means need more bodies on floor level.
- Play slower, more methodical basement clear without vertical rush advantage.

**Who calls it:** Peej calls "hatches denied, ground-only approach" after first hatch attempt fails.

---

### Win C — Basement Corridors Heavily Fortified ⚠️
**When:** Defenders anchor heavily in Red Hallway / Lockers with strong reinforcements, blocking direct approach.

**Adjustment:**
- Do not push the main Lockers Hallway if it's a fortress. Use Blue Stairs approach instead (1F Square → 1F Blue Stairs → 0F Server).
- Or use vertical hatches as **primary entry point** — drop directly into Server from above, bypassing corridor approach entirely.
- Coordinate with vertical team to time hatch destruction exactly when 3-man approaches stairs to create simultaneous pressure.
- If both routes are shut: play for picks and attacker spawn control. Reset and try again with different approach.

**Who calls it:** Sarge calls "corridors locked, going Blue Stairs" or Grant calls "going vertical-first, stacking hatches."

---

### Win D — Early Man Advantage (Support Gets Pick)
**When:** Grant or Peej gets one or more kills during setup/approach, creating a 5v4 or 5v3.

**Adjustment:**
- **Accelerate execute** — do not wait for full drone readiness. Push faster to capitalize on advantage.
- If vertical player gets kill: drop hatch immediately and maintain pressure from above.
- If 3-man gets basement kill: commit to breach without waiting for full utility setup.
- Play aggressively once advantage is confirmed. Defenders are thinner.

**Who calls it:** Grant/Peej calls every kill. Team pushes on man-advantage call immediately.

---

## Audibles

- **Basement corridors impassable / too many anchors:** Pivot to Blue Stairs approach via 1F Square, or full vertical-first strategy.
- **Both vertical players (Peej/Grant) dead early:** Go to ground-only 3-man execute. No overhead support — push harder at ground level.
- **Utility fully denied / no EMPs left:** Play for picks on approach. Do not push a fully fortified basement without utility support.
- **Roamer kills Smigs during approach:** Call it. Go to 3-man soft breach or retreat and reset. No hard breach utility = force a different execute.

strats.siteContext: 1F bomb site featuring two distinct large rooms (Archives and Open Space) with multiple approach vectors and vertical hatches. Archives is positioned north with floor destruction enabling 2F CEO pressure. Open Space is central with direct Kitchen/Admin connections and ceiling hatch to basement. Multiple approach angles and vertical access create execution flexibility. Site rewards methodical map control before breach.
strats.content:
# Bank — Archives / Open Space (1F) · ATTACK

**Status:** ⚠️ Partially Developed
**Formation:** 3-2
**Developed:** 2026-03-14
**Data Source:** STACK_03 migration
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej replaces Slug. Name swap only — confirm role assignments when this strat gets reps.

> **Site Context:** 1F bomb site featuring two distinct large rooms (Archives and Open Space) with multiple approach vectors and vertical hatches. Archives is positioned north with floor destruction enabling 2F CEO pressure. Open Space is central with direct Kitchen/Admin connections and ceiling hatch to basement. Multiple approach angles and vertical access create execution flexibility. Site rewards methodical map control before breach.

---

## Spawn

3-man and 2-man groups spawn to enable map control spread. Coordinate to control Square (vertical/northeastern hub) and main stairwell approach simultaneously.

---

## Roles

| Player | Role | Operator | Group |
|--------|------|----------|-------|
| **Hound** | Vertical / Map Control | Flex | 3-man |
| **Sarge** | Vertical / Entry | Flex | 3-man |
| **Smigs** | Map Control / Support | Flex | 3-man |
| **Peej** | Flex / First floor flex | Flex | 2-man |
| **Grant** | Flex / First floor flex | Flex | 2-man |

**Hound** — Upstairs through Square/Stock with Sarge and Smigs. Play vertical, take map control. Supports vertical pressure on 1F from above (2F). Denies roamer rotations.

**Sarge** — Upstairs through Square/Stock with Hound and Smigs. Play vertical, take map control. First entry through primary approach once site is cleared. Likely plants.

**Smigs** — Upstairs through Square/Stock with Sarge and Hound. Watch flanks, take map control. Utility support on approach. Anti-gadget coverage.

**Peej** — 1F flex role. Drone effectively. Cut off map to suffocate defenders into site. Manages 1F Lobby/Tellers area approach. Control Loan/ATM area to prevent sneaky rotations.

**Grant** — 1F flex role. Drone effectively. Cut off map to suffocate defenders into site. Manages 1F main hallway and Kitchen side. Prevents defender flanks from unexpected angles.

---

## Execute Sequence

1. **Drone phase** — Grant/Peej drone 1F Lobby, Tellers, and main hallway to identify anchor positions. 3-man drones upper approach through Stock/Meeting/Top Square areas. Identify roamer positions.

2. **1F perimeter control** — Peej/Grant establish control of Lobby, Tellers, Loan areas. Cut off main 1F rotations. Force defenders to commit to site early.

3. **Upper approach** — 3-man moves through 2F Stock/Meeting/Top Square area. Take high ground and establish vertical control. Prepare for simultaneous 1F pressure below.

4. **1F main clear** — Peej/Grant methodically clear Kitchen, Admin, and main hallway connections. Deny roamer flanks. Establish 1F control before site execute.

5. **Archives approach** — 3-man and 1F team coordinate simultaneous pressure on Archives from multiple angles (Square stairs, Kitchen connection, main hall).

6. **Open Space pressure** — Parallel pressure on Open Space from multiple doors (Hallway, Kitchen, Admin). Don't let defenders anchor safely — create crossfire.

7. **Vertical destruction** — Destroy Archives floor (to 2F CEO) and Open Space ceiling hatch once pressure confirmed. Expand breach opportunities.

8. **Plant** — Plant in Archives or Open Space depending on anchor positions. Protect from roamer re-entry and rotation pressure.

---

## Plant Position (PP)

**Primary:** Archives — back corner or far wall (protected from Kitchen approach and main hallway entry)
**Alternate:** Open Space center or far corner if Archives too contested; Open Space ceiling hatch provides coverage from above

---

## Post-Plant

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Sarge** | Archives / Open Space junction | Holds cross angles; watches for Kitchen roamer counter-push |
| **Hound** | Top Square / above site | Maintains vertical control; prevents defender return from 2F |
| **Smigs** | Main hallway / Kitchen side | Watches for rotation pressure from 1F perimeter |
| **Peej** | Lobby side / kitchen rotation | Protects backside; watches for Loan/Tellers roamer return |
| **Grant** | Perimeter control | Watches secondary flanks; adapts to defender rotation attempts |

---

## Watch For

- **Square roamer (if present)** — Square is the vertical hub connecting 1F and 2F. Defenders may position here early. 3-man should drone Square thoroughly before committing. If roamer is spotted, pressure Square aggressively before he rotates away.

- **Kitchen anchored defender** — Expected setup. If strong anchor is in Kitchen, focus Open Space approach first. Don't let defenders have time to dig in.

- **Aruni gate on Archives doorframe** — Smigs covers anti-gadget. If Aruni blocks approach, use Kitchen destruction route or fall back to Open Space-first strategy.

- **Bandit / Kaid on Archives/Open Space walls** — Expected on critical doorframes. Hound EMPs first. If wall is tricked with no EMP left, pivot to alternate entry.

- **Lesion (Gu mines)** — Seeded in Square approach and Kitchen connections. Ping tells Lesion the 3-man position. Move with intent through approach corridors.

- **Mira Black Mirror** — May be on Archives or Open Space wall. Breaching opens wall and destroys mirror. Call it immediately.

- **Fenrir (F-NATT gas)** — Canisters can block plant spots in Open Space center. Use grenade utility to clear before planting.

- **Tachanka (Shumikha Grenade)** — Can create fire denial zones in Open Space or Kitchen. If grenades block approach, wait for fire to fade or use alternate route.

- **Admin/Kitchen side anchor** — Defenders may consolidate in Admin or Kitchen instead of site. Expect this setup. Peej/Grant should pressure Kitchen early to flush defender out or isolate them.

---

## Win Conditions

There is more than one way to win this site. The execute sequence above is **Win A**. If conditions change mid-round, call the audible early — don't burn time forcing a denied path.

---

### Win A — Primary: Vertical Stack + Multi-Angle Ground Pressure ✅
**When:** Upper approach is accessible, 1F is clear, multiple pressure vectors available.

3-man establishes vertical dominance while Peej/Grant execute simultaneous ground-level pressure. Multiple angles overwhelm defenders. Plant in Archives or Open Space.

**Who calls it:** Grant calls 1F clear. Sarge calls upper approach ready. Simultaneous commitment on both callouts.

---

### Win B — Square / Upper Approach Contested ⚠️
**When:** Roamer controls Square or upper approach is heavily defended; vertical pressure route is blocked.

**Adjustment:**
- Abandon upper approach. Focus on ground-level 1F execute only.
- 3-man comes down stairs immediately and joins 1F team for coordinated site take.
- Shift to pure ground-level 4v3 or 5v3 pressure (3-man + 2 1F flex = 5 on site).
- Use Kitchen/Admin routes heavily instead of relying on vertical.
- Open Space becomes primary — less roamer-accessible than Archives.

**Who calls it:** Hound calls "Square locked, going ground-only" after confirming contested upper approach.

---

### Win C — Kitchen Heavily Fortified ⚠️
**When:** Strong anchor digs into Kitchen with reinforcements; multiple roamers defend Kitchen side.

**Adjustment:**
- Avoid Kitchen entirely. Focus Archives approach via Square → Admin → Archives.
- Open Space becomes secondary — use Open Space as byproduct of Archives take.
- If Kitchen is super-fortified: let it be. Destroy Archives first, plant there, then deal with Kitchen rotation as roamer threat, not site anchor.
- Peej/Grant focus Lobby/Loan perimeter to cut off flanks while 3-man commits to Archives.

**Who calls it:** Peej calls "Kitchen locked, going Archives-only" after aggressive approach fails.

---

### Win D — Early Man Advantage (Support Gets Pick)
**When:** Peej or Grant gets one or more kills during 1F control phase, creating a 5v4 or 5v3.

**Adjustment:**
- **Push tempo immediately** — do not wait for full drone or upper team setup.
- If 1F flex gets kill: commit to site execute now. 3-man accelerates upper approach.
- Use man-advantage to force plant faster. Play aggressively.
- Defenders are thinner — don't give them time to reset positions.

**Who calls it:** Peej/Grant calls every kill. Full team pushes on man-advantage call.

---

## Audibles

- **Square inaccessible from above / too many roamers:** Entire 3-man comes down stairs to ground-level 1F execute.
- **Both 1F flex players (Peej/Grant) dead early:** 3-man must clear 1F solo. Slower execute, more cautious. Play for utility denial and control.
- **Kitchen roamer gets early kills:** Fall back from Kitchen approach. Use Admin destruction route instead of Kitchen pressure.
- **Utility fully denied / no EMPs left on approach:** Play for picks and map control. Reset and try again. Don't force a breached wall with no utility support.

overviewContent:
# Bank — Overview

**Environment:** Urban bank in Los Angeles, California; heavily fortified Prestige National Bank downtown financial institution
**Floors:** Basement/0F, Ground Floor/1F, Top Floor/2F + Exterior & Roof
**In Y11S1 Ranked Pool:** ✅ Yes — first half of season
**Year Added / Reworked:** Y1 S1 (2015, original); full art rework Y6S3 Crystal Guard (September 2021)

> 📌 **Y11S1 Note:** Bank remains in the ranked pool for the full Y11S1 season. The map emphasizes verticality and hatch play — key strengths for coordinated team play.

---

## Overview

Bank is a verticality-heavy map centered around the three-story Prestige National Bank building in Los Angeles. The map emphasizes hatch play and multi-floor coordination, with distinctive features including the iconic Skylight hatch above 2F CEO Office, basement Vault with single-entry defense, and complex basement corridor network. Bank rewards coordinated vertical pressure and careful destruction sequencing. Four bomb site options create flexible strategies. The Crystal Guard rework (Y6S3) significantly altered exterior angles, removed vault metal bars for improved destruction, blocked Low Roof skylights, adjusted Back Alley sightlines, and updated spawn positions. The map remains a cornerstone competitive selection.

Stack performance on Bank is strong. Grant posts a 1.47 K/D with solid round wins; Slug maintained 1.35 K/D (B Team as of 2026-03-21; Peej data pending). Most stack members maintain 55–65% win rate. The team has developed 4 full strategies across the four bomb sites, indicating strong map familiarity and adaptability. Vault site is the strongest defensive anchor point and correlates with team success on defense. The Skylight hatch is well-understood by the team, and attackers execute predictable but effective vertical pressure. No major weakness identified; Bank is considered a skill-check map where execution matters.

---

## Bomb Sites

### Site 1 — CEO Office / Teller's Office — 2F
**Rooms:** CEO Office and Teller's Office
**Description:** 2F bomb site with two distinct rooms. CEO Office is a giant central room with large desk; directly vulnerable to Skylight hatch above and floor destruction from 1F Archives. Teller's Office is a long room featuring bank teller counter windows with views into 1F Tellers; destructible walls connect to main circulation and floor destruction enables 1F Teller sightlines. Surrounded by destructible walls and doors to Executive Lounge (west), Exec Hallway (north), and Janitor (south).
**Common defense:** Anchor holds CEO behind desk for strong cover; Executive Lounge anchor supports via door angles. Secondary anchor can position in Teller's counter for window angles. Roamers control Banana hallway and Stock Hallway for stairwell/vertical pressure denial. Multiple reinforcements needed to segment objective.
**Attack approach:** See ATK_CEO_Tellers.md

### Site 2 — Archives / Open Space — 1F
**Rooms:** Archives (large north-center room) and Open Space (large central room)
**Description:** 1F bomb site featuring two distinct large rooms with multiple approach vectors. Archives is positioned north with connections to Square and main hallways; destructible floor enables 2F CEO pressure. Open Space is the central room connected to Kitchen, Printer, Admin through multiple doors/destructible walls; floor hatch drops to 0F Lockers Hallway. Each room has distinct entry vectors and rotations.
**Common defense:** Anchor positioning varies — can hold from Archive corners or Open Space center depending on objective. Roamers position in Square, Lobby corners, and main stairwell areas. Atrium/Square becomes critical roamer zone for all 1F sites.
**Attack approach:** See ATK_Staff_OpenArea.md

### Site 3 — Tellers / Tellers' Office — 1F
**Rooms:** Tellers' Office (long counter room) and Tellers (curved teller counter area)
**Description:** 1F features teller-focused bomb objective locations. Tellers' Office is long room with curved teller windows and connection to Lobby; destructible floor enables 2F Teller sightlines. Tellers curved area is the exposed teller counter with direct Lobby connection. Primary access from Banana stairs via floor destruction or Lobby approach.
**Common defense:** Anchor positioning holds from Teller's counter windows. Roamers manage Lobby corners and Banana stairwell. Window angles into Lobby critical for defense.
**Attack approach:** See ATK_Archives_Tellers.md

### Site 4 — Vault / Server Room — Basement (0F)
**Rooms:** Vault (heavily reinforced room) and Server Room (large northeast room with pillars)
**Description:** Basement features vault-focused and server-focused bomb objective locations. Vault is iconic heavily-reinforced room with single red-carpeted entrance via Vault Entrance room; minimal destruction options. Server Room is large northeast room with pillars and cover; accessible from basement corridor network and ceiling hatches. Ceiling hatches from 1F Open Space, Admin, and Elevators enable vertical pressure on all basement sites.
**Common defense:** Vault anchor holds exterior door with angled coverage; single entry makes it most defensible basement location. Server Room anchor positions among pillars for cover. Roamers manage basement corridor network (Red Hallway, Lockers Hallway) and upper basement stairwell monitoring.
**Attack approach:** See ATK_Servers_CCTV.md

---

## Room Names & Community Callouts

### 2F (Top Floor) — Verified names

| Stock Hallway | Stock Hall / East Hall | Eastern hallway. |
| 2F Elevators | Elevators / Elev | West elevator only. |
| Terrace | Terrace | Exterior wooden platform. |

### 1F (Ground Floor) — Verified names

| Side Alley | Side Alley | Alley between Boulevard and Back Alley. |
| 2F Terrace | Terrace | Large wooden exterior platform on 2F. |

---

## Key Walls & Hatches

### Critical Reinforced Walls (prioritize reinforcing)
- **Vault Entry Door (Basement):** Single point of entry to Vault bomb site; heavily fortified; primary defense anchor
- **CEO to Executive Lounge wall (2F):** Separates primary bomb room from support room; divides site defense
- **CEO to Exec Hallway wall (2F):** Controls north entrance to CEO; key chokepoint
- **Server Room walls (Basement):** Multiple destruction walls around Server; reinforce to prevent wrapping
- **Lockers to surrounding rooms (Basement):** Multiple reinforcements control Lockers bomb site access
- **Tellers' Office walls (1F):** Destruction walls to main areas; control access to teller objective

### Key Destructible/Soft Walls (critical for vertical play)
- **Skylight hatch opening above CEO Office (2F):** Massive destructible hatch; primary vertical pressure point; usually destroyed early
- **CEO Office floor (2F):** Destructible floor enables gunfights to 1F Archives
- **Tellers' Office floor (2F Banana):** Curved hallway floor directly above 1F Tellers; destructible for vertical angles
- **Tellers' Office floor (1F):** Destructible roof enables 2F Teller sightlines
- **Archives floor (1F):** Destructible roof to 2F CEO; critical vertical connection
- **Top Square floor (2F):** Destructible floor to 1F Square; vertical pressure point
- **Open Space roof (1F):** Destructible ceiling to 2F Stock; vertical destruction opportunity
- **Stock Trading Room floor (2F):** Destructible floor to 1F Open Space
- **Admin ceiling (1F):** Destructible to 2F Janitor
- **Red Hallway walls (Basement):** Longitudinal and latitudinal destruction walls; basement corridor control
- **Lockers Hallway connections (Basement):** Destruction walls to Lockers/Red Hallway/CCTV

### Key Hatches (8 total)
- **Skylight hatch (above 2F CEO):** Massive vertical pressure point; roof access
- **2F Banana to 1F Tellers (destructible floor):** Mid-map vertical connection; roamer rotation route
- **Top Square to 1F Square (destructible floor/staircase hatch):** Large stairwell; vertical access
- **1F Open Space to 0F Lockers Hallway (floor hatch):** Basement vertical pressure point
- **1F Admin to 0F CCTV (floor hatch):** Admin area basement connection
- **1F Elevators to 0F Elevators (floor/ceiling hatches):** Vertical elevator shaft access
- **2F Elevators to 1F Elevators (ceiling hatch):** West elevator only; vertical connection
- **1F Square to 0F Server (ceiling hatch):** Server room vertical access; critical basement pressure

---

## Stack Performance Notes (Y10S4)

| Player | Operator | Win Rate | K/D | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Grant | — | Strong | 1.47 | Solid round wins; strong K/D performance |
| Peej | — | 80.0% | 1.71 | Y11S1: 5M / 80.0% / K/D 1.71 — Strong — top performer on Bank Y11S1 |
| Slug | — | Strong | 1.35 | Consistent high K/D *(B Team as of 2026-03-21; historical data)* |
| Team Average | — | 55–65% | — | Maintained across most players |

**Notes:** Bank is a strong map for the team. Team has developed 4 full strategies across the four bomb sites, indicating strong map familiarity and adaptability. Vault site is the strongest defensive anchor point and correlates with team success on defense. The Skylight hatch is well-understood by the team, and attackers execute predictable but effective vertical pressure. No major weakness identified; Bank is considered a skill-check map where execution matters.

---

_Confidence: ✅ High — Bank room names, hatch locations (8 total), bomb site configurations, and floor/wall destruction points verified via floor plan images and Rainbow Six Wiki. All exterior spawn locations, interior circulation, and camera positions confirmed. Crystal Guard rework changes documented._

---

| Site 2 | Archives | Open Space | 1F |

---

## 2F — Top Floor Layout

| Room | Adjacent Rooms | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **CEO Office** | Executive Lounge (west), Exec Hallway (north), Janitor (south), destructible floor to 1F Archives | Bomb site (Site 1A). Large central room with desk. Skylight hatch above. Visible from Exec areas. |
| **Teller's Office** | Banana (west), main circulation (south), destructible floor to 1F Tellers | Bomb site (Site 1B). Long room with curved teller counter windows. Views into 1F Tellers curved area. |
| **Executive Lounge** | CEO (east), Front Desk (south), doors/destruction to Meeting/CEO | Support room west of CEO. Door angles into CEO. Multiple destruction options. |
| **Exec Hallway** | CEO (south), Banana (west) | Small hallway connecting Banana to CEO area. Chokepoint access. |
| **2F Banana** | Teller's (east), Executive Lounge (west), Lobby Stairs (south), destructible floor to 1F Tellers | Curved hallway directly above 1F Tellers. Destructible floor enables vertical gunfights. |
| **Front Office / Front Desk** | Executive Lounge (north), destruction to Exec/Meeting areas, Terrace (exterior) | Western room. Accessible from Banana via window/door. Exterior balcony access. |
| **Stock Trading Room** | Meeting Hallway (south), Terrace (exterior), ceiling hatch to 1F Open Space | Large south room. Doors to Terrace. Ceiling hatch is vertical pressure point. |
| **Janitor Closet** | CEO (north), Stock Hallway (south), drone vents to Stock Hallway, ceiling hatch to 1F Admin | Central angled room. Drone vent connections. Ceiling hatch is secondary vertical point. |
| **2F Top Square** | Stock Hallway (south), Meeting Hallway (west), Main Stairs (south), destructible floor to 1F Square | Large square room. Skylight stairwell area. Destructible floor to 1F Square. Vertical access. |
| **Meeting Room** | Meeting Hallway (west), doors to Exec/CEO areas | Central rectangle with conference table. Connected to multiple hallways. |
| **Meeting Hallway** | Stock Hallway (east), Meeting Room (north), Elevators (south), Banana (west), stairs junction (south) | Western hallway connecting multiple rooms and stairwell. Main circulation corridor. |
| **Stock Hallway** | Meeting Hallway (west), Top Square (north), Stock Room (west), Janitor (west) | Eastern hallway. Connects to central stairwell area. Access to Janitor/Stock. |
| **2F Elevators** | Meeting Hallway (north), ceiling hatch to 1F Elevators | West elevator room only. Floor hatch to 1F. Limited access point. |
| **Terrace** | Front Office (north), Stock (east), exterior | Large wooden external area. Accessible from 2F windows. Exterior rappel routes. |

---

## 1F — Ground Floor Layout

| Room | Adjacent Rooms | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **ATMs** | Lobby (east/south), Main Entrance (north) | First interior room from Main Entrance. ATM displays. Entry transition. |
| **1F Lobby** | ATMs (west), Tellers (south), Open Space Hallway (east), Loan (south), Main Stairs (north), destructible floor to 0F Vault | Large central hub. Columns on west side. Multiple doors. Skylight hatch visible. |
| **1F Tellers** | Tellers' Office (east/north), Lobby (west), curved wall area | Curved wall teller counter windows. Views into Tellers' Office. Exposed area. |
| **Tellers' Office** | Tellers (west), Lobby (west/south), doors/destruction to main areas, destructible floor to 2F Banana | Long room bomb objective. Teller counter. Destructible floor to 2F. |
| **Open Space Hallway** | Lobby (west), Elevators (west), Loan (south), Open Space (east) | Hallway connecting Lobby to Open/Loan areas. Camera position. Drone vent to Tellers. |
| **1F Open Space** | Kitchen (north), Hallway (west), Printer (east), Admin (north), doors/destruction, ceiling hatch to 0F Lockers, destructible floor to 2F Stock | Large central bomb room. Multiple door/destruction accesses. Vertical pressure point. |
| **Admin Office** | Kitchen (south), Open Space (south), destructible walls to Kitchen/Archives (north/east), ceiling hatch to 0F CCTV | Small room with destruction options. Ceiling hatch connection. Roamer flex zone. |
| **1F Kitchen** | Admin (south), Open Space (west), Square (north), Printer (east), doors/destruction | Large room east side. Multiple connections. Bomb objective adjacent area. |
| **Printer Room** | Kitchen (west), Open Space (north/west), exterior window to Alley, drone vent | Small southeast room. Exterior window. Alley access. |
| **1F Square** | Kitchen (south), Archives (west), Lobby (west/south via stairs), destructible floor to 2F Top Square, ceiling hatch to 0F Server | Large northeast room. Stairwell hub. Vertical access both up and down. Multiple connections. |
| **Electrical Room** | Kitchen (west), exterior door to Alley | Small room adjacent to Kitchen. Limited access. Alley connection. |
| **Archives** | Square (east), Admin (south), destruction to Tellers (west), doors to main hallway | Large north-center room. Bomb objective. Destruction walls. Multiple access points. |
| **Loan Office** | Lobby (north), exterior window/views to Garage Roof | Southern room. Limited connections. Exterior windows. |
| **1F Elevators** | Open Space Hallway (east), ceiling hatch to 2F Elevators, floor hatch to 0F Elevators | Two small elevator spaces. Vertical access point. |
| **Lobby Stairs** | Lobby (south), 2F Banana (north) | Small stairwell. Connects Lobby to 2F Banana. Secondary stair route. |
| **Main Stairway** | Lobby (north), Open Space Hallway (east), 2F Meeting Hallway (north), 0F Vault Lobby (down) | Central stairwell. Connects all three floors. Main circulation spine. |

---

## Basement (0F) Layout

| Room | Adjacent Rooms | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **Garage / Dock** | Vault Lobby (north), Lockers Hallway (east), Main Stairs (south), doors to exterior | Large parking garage area. Red trucks present. Dock structure. Defender spawn area nearby. |

| **Vault Entrance** | Vault Lobby (south), Vault (north), destruction to Lockers (east) | Red-carpeted room. Door to Vault. Single entry control point. Reinforced. |
| **Vault** | Vault Entrance (south), Gold Vault (north), ceiling hatch to 1F Lobby | Bomb objective. Iconic heavily-reinforced room. Single door entry. Strong anchor position. |
| **Gold Vault** | Vault (south), Red Hallway (south), door connections | Northern room. Gold bars/cash displays. Vault support area. North corner. |
| **Red Hallway** | Gold Vault (north), Lockers (south), CCTV (east), destruction walls (north/south/east) | Northern basement corridor. Longitudinal destruction options. Multiple connections. |
| **Lockers Hallway** | Vault Lobby (west), Lockers (north), Red Hallway (north), CCTV (east), ceiling hatch from 1F Open Space | Southern basement corridor. Central east-west passage. Hatch connection to Open Space. |
| **Lockers** | Lockers Hallway (south), Red Hallway (north), Vault Entrance (west), destruction walls to surrounding | Central basement bomb room. Multiple destruction options. Server Room connection (via CCTV). |
| **CCTV Room** | Lockers (west/north), Lockers Hallway (south), Red Hallway (north), Server (east), destruction to Red Hallway/Server | Security area bomb objective. East-central basement. Door and destruction connections. |
| **Server Room** | CCTV (west), Blue Stairs (south), Tunnel (north), ceiling hatch from 1F Square, doors to Tunnel/Blue Stairs | Large northeast room with pillars. Bomb objective. Hatch connection from above. Multiple pressure points. |
| **Blue Stairs** | Server (north), 1F Square (up), small connection room | Small room. Door to Server. Stairwell to 1F Square. Secondary vertical access. |
| **Tunnel** | Exterior Jewelry manhole (entrance), Server (south), ladder access | Narrow sewer tunnel from exterior to Server Room. Attacker approach route. Limited defender access. |
| **0F Elevators** | Main Stairs (south), 1F Elevators (up), ceiling hatch | Elevator space. Vertical access point. Limited room. |
| **Main Stairway** | Vault Lobby (north), Garage/Dock (south), 1F connection (up), 0F connection (down) | Central stairwell. Connects basement to 1F and 2F. Main vertical spine. |

---

## Key Adjacencies by Bomb Site

### Site 1 — CEO Office / Teller's Office (2F)
**Attacker approach corridors:** Executive Lounge (→ CEO door), Exec Hallway (→ CEO north), Banana (→ Teller's direct), Meeting Hallway (→ CEO via Meeting Room)
**Defender anchor zones:** CEO behind desk, Teller's counter windows, Executive Lounge support angles
**Vertical access / Skylight:** Skylight hatch directly above CEO — primary vertical destruction point. Floor destruction CEO→1F Archives, Banana→1F Tellers
**Roamer territory:** Banana, hallway junctions, Top Square approach

### Site 2 — Archives / Open Space (1F)
**Attacker approach rooms:** Square (→ Archives direct), Open Space Hallway (→ Open Space), Kitchen (→ Open Space north), Admin (→ both via destruction)
**Defender anchor positions:** Archives corners, Open Space center, Admin flex
**Vertical access:** Ceiling hatches from 2F Stock (→ Open Space), Janitor (→ Admin)
**Roamer territory:** Square, Lobby, Kitchen, Admin areas

### Site 3 — Tellers / Tellers' Office (1F)
**Attacker approach rooms:** Banana (→ Teller's floor destruction), Tellers curved area (→ Teller's direct), Lobby (→ Tellers via Lobby Stairs)
**Defender anchor positions:** Teller's counter, Banana support
**Vertical access:** Teller's floor destructible to 2F Banana, Banana floor to 1F Tellers
**Roamer territory:** Lobby, Banana upper, Main Stairs

### Site 4 — Vault / Server Room (Basement)
**Attacker approach rooms:** Vault Lobby (→ Vault Entrance → Vault), Lockers (→ Server/CCTV), Blue Stairs (→ Server), Tunnel (→ Server exterior entry)
**Defender anchor positions:** Vault door interior, Server among pillars, CCTV security
**Vertical access:** Multiple ceiling hatches (1F Square→Server, Open Space→Lockers, Admin→CCTV, Lobby→Vault)
**Basement corridor control:** Red Hallway, Lockers Hallway for rotation and defense

---

## Vertical Relationships (1F ↔ 2F, 2F ↔ B)

| 2F Room | Roughly Above (1F) | Connection Type |

| CEO Office | Archives | Destructible floor (upward pressure) |

| Lobby | Vault | Ceiling hatch (direct overhead) |
| Open Space | Lockers Hallway | Floor hatch (basement vertical) |
| Admin | CCTV | Floor hatch (basement vertical) |
| 1F Elevators | 0F Elevators | Floor/Ceiling hatch (elevator shaft) |
| 1F Square | Server Room | Ceiling hatch (basement vertical) |

> ⚠️ Vertical relationships approximate based on floor plan overlay. Confirm exact hatch positions in-game — hatches visible as marked openings on floor plans.

---

## Staircase Reference

| Name | Location | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|
| **Main Stairway / Main Stairs** | 1F Lobby area → 2F Meeting Hallway, 0F Vault Lobby | Central stairwell. Connects all three floors. Primary vertical spine. Camera coverage. |

strats.siteContext: 2F north bomb site. Primary attacker approach via Yellow Stairs (central) from Main Lobby. Exterior catwalks and windows provide secondary pressure routes.
strats.content:
# Border — Armory Lockers / Archives (2F) · ATTACK

**Status:** ✅ Fully Developed
**Formation:** 4-1
**Developed:** 2026-03-14 (migrated from STACK_03)
**Site Context:** 2F north bomb site. Primary attacker approach via Yellow Stairs (central) from Main Lobby. Exterior catwalks and windows provide secondary pressure routes.

---

## Spawn

3-man group spawns Valley (central). Two solos spawn East or West depending on vertical/exterior balance needs. Adjust spawn split based on ban phase.

---

## Roles

| Player | Role | Operator | Group |
|--------|------|----------|-------|
| **Hound** | Shield / Map Control | Shield / Map Control | With Peej (Walkway approach) |
| **Sarge** | Map Control / Anti-gadget | Map Control / Anti-gadget | With Smigs (Window approach) |
| **Smigs** | Map Control / Anti-gadget | Map Control / Anti-gadget | With Sarge (Window approach) |
| **Peej** | Shield / Map Control | Shield / Map Control | With Hound (Walkway approach) |
| **Grant** | Flex | Flex | Backside / underneath rotation |

**Hound (Shield/Map Control)** — Walkway with Peej. Play passive early; allow Window team to set up. If shield: ready to plant on call. If map control: watch flanks and long angles post-plant.

**Sarge (Map Control/Anti-gadget)** — Window with Smigs. Set up for plant, cut off site access as much as possible to allow time for Shield team or map control operator.

**Smigs (Map Control/Anti-gadget)** — Window with Sarge. Set up for plant, cut off site access, support utility against defender gadgets.

**Peej (Shield/Map Control)** — Walkway with Hound. Play passive early; allow others to set up. If shield: ready to plant. If map control: watch flanks and long angles post-plant.

**Grant (Flex)** — Play backside or underneath site depending on defense. Catch defenders off guard, get man advantage early while plant is going down.

---

## Execute Sequence

1. **Drone phase** — Grant advances to backside/underneath position and drones to confirm roamer/anchor locations. Walkway team (Hound/Peej) drones Yellow Stairs approach. Window team (Sarge/Smigs) drones external windows and Archives side.

2. **Setup** — Walkway team (Hound/Peej) positions for Yellow Stairs push. Window team (Sarge/Smigs) positions for exterior window pressure. Grant confirms backside/underneath access.

3. **Initial push** — Walkway team moves into Yellow Stairs area with shield/utility. Window team applies exterior window pressure. Grant moves into position.

4. **Utility denial** — Anti-gadget operators (Sarge/Smigs) destroy Kapkan traps on stairs, deny gadget positions, clear debris.

5. **Site entry** — Walkway team enters Armory/Archives from Yellow Stairs. Window team provides covering fire or follows through secondary angle.

6. **Site clear** — Establish control of Armory Lockers and Archives. Clear remaining defenders; identify safe plant locations.

7. **Plant** — Plant in Office/Fountain area or deeper Archives position depending on defender positions. Walkway operator or Grant executes plant.

8. **Post-plant** — Team secures perimeter, holds cross angles, denies defuser plays.

---

## Plant Position (PP)

**Primary:** Office/Fountain area — central plant with cover from multiple angles
**Alternate:** Deep Archives — if site pressure is asymmetrically defended

---

## Post-Plant

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Hound** | Armory/site depth | Holds cover position; last anchor |
| **Sarge** | Site perimeter | Holds cross angles; denies rotations |
| **Smigs** | Site perimeter | Covers secondary angles; supports primary defense |
| **Peej** | Stair denial / flank watch | Prevents re-entry from Yellow Stairs; watches flanks |
| **Grant** | Backside/underneath | Holds rotation denial; prevents flanking |

---

## Watch For

- **Kapkan at Yellow Stairs top:** Most dangerous trap placement. Hound/Sarge must drone and clear before pushing. Loss of utility to traps = loss of execute momentum.

- **Aruni at north site entries:** Gate on Armory Lockers main entry from Admin side will damage team pushing through. Destroy or hack if available.

- **Stair control from Break Room:** Roamer may hold stairs from Break Room side. Listen for audio cues before committing to full site push.

- **Exterior window defenders:** Window-side roamers may hold Armory/Archives from exterior facing angles. Sarge/Smigs must confirm window pressure before committing.

- **Valkyrie cameras:** Look for cams on Yellow Stairs landing, exterior windows, Long Hallway approaches. Destroy early if spotted.

- **Roamer from Small Hall / Fountain area:** Secondary defender may flank through Small Hall into north site. Grant's backside confirms roamer commit before team pushes.

---

## Win Conditions

### Win A — Primary: Yellow Stairs Clear Push ✅
**When:** Yellow Stairs roamer is eliminated or denied, Walkway team gains entry, utility is ready.

Primary execute through central Yellow Stairs. Shield and utility deny Kapkan, Walkway team sets up for plant. Sarge/Smigs provide window cover or secondary push.

**Who calls it:** Hound calls "Clear" once Stairs are confirmed safe. Grant confirms no roamer backside.

---

### Win B — Window Pressure Forces Rotation
**When:** External window pressure is strong, defenders rotate to defend exterior, Walkway team exploits opening.

Sarge/Smigs apply sustained exterior window pressure. Defenders commit to exterior defense. Walkway team exploits gap and pushes site. Plant executes faster than normal due to defender split.

**Who calls it:** Sarge calls sustained pressure; Hound exploits rotation gap.

---

### Win C — Backside Chaos
**When:** Grant gets early backside kill or establishes man advantage before execute. Site pressure decreases.

Grant's backside kill creates 5v4 or better. Walkway team pushes harder knowing site is thinner. Plant goes down faster.

**Who calls it:** Grant announces kill; team accelerates execute immediately.

---

### Win D — Stair Access Denied
**When:** Yellow Stairs is shut down completely (Kapkan kills, defender dense), or too much utility spent clearing.

**Adjustment:** Shift to exterior window approach as primary. Sarge/Smigs breach through windows. Hound/Peej reposition to support window team. Walkway becomes secondary distraction.

**Who calls it:** Hound calls "Stairs denied, going window primary."

---

## Audibles

- **Heavy Kapkan / trap setup:** Abort high-risk Stairs push. Go window-first instead.
- **Roamer kills Walkway operator early:** Shift utility to Window team. Make Window the primary execute instead.
- **Grant dies before entry:** Play 4v5. Tighten team around plant; don't chase roamer.
- **Both shield operators down:** No plant setup possible — play for picks and utility advantage, reset round.

---

## Formation Notes

The 4-1 formation splits the team into two distinct pressure vectors: Walkway (Hound/Peej) and Window (Sarge/Smigs), with Grant as the chaos variable. Flexibility is key — adjust to defender positioning. If Stairs pressure is denied, pivot to Window. If exterior is locked down, pivot to Stairs. Grant's job is to create imbalance and capitalize on defender splits.

---

## Development Notes

strats.siteContext: 2F east bomb site. Two hatches from Security Room to 1F Customs areas enable dual vertical pressure. Primary approach via Red Stairs (east), secondary via Long Hallway from Admin.
strats.content:
# Border — Offices / Security Room (2F) · ATTACK

**Status:** ✅ Fully Developed
**Formation:** 3-2
**Developed:** 2026-03-14 (migrated from STACK_03)
**Site Context:** 2F east bomb site. Two hatches from Security Room to 1F Customs areas enable dual vertical pressure. Primary approach via Red Stairs (east), secondary via Long Hallway from Admin.

---

## Spawn

3-man group spawns Valley (central). Two solos spawn East (for vertical pressure setup). Adjust spawn split based on vertical vs. direct approach needs.

---

## Roles

| Player | Role | Operator | Group |
|--------|------|----------|-------|
| **Hound** | Anti-gadget | Anti-gadget | With Smigs (Ventilation approach) |
| **Sarge** | Map Control / Flex | Map Control / Flex | With Smigs and Hound (Ventilation approach) |
| **Smigs** | Hard Breach | Hard Breach | With Hound and Sarge (Ventilation approach) |
| **Peej** | Vert / Flex | Vert / Flex | With Grant (Upstairs approach) |
| **Grant** | Vert / Flex | Vert / Flex | With Peej (Upstairs approach) |

**Hound (Anti-gadget)** — Ventilation group with Smigs and Sarge. Push into Workshop, get bathroom wall for plant setup. Deny Kapkan/gadgets on approach.

**Sarge (Map Control/Flex)** — Ventilation group with Hound and Smigs. Push into Workshop, cut off flanks and rotations from site.

**Smigs (Hard Breach)** — Ventilation group with Hound and Sarge. Push into Workshop, establish entry for plant setup. Hard breach utility on critical walls.

**Peej (Vert/Flex)** — Upstairs with Grant. Establish crosses, eliminate or cut off roamers on 2F. Create space for Security Room push.

**Grant (Vert/Flex)** — Upstairs with Peej. Establish crosses, eliminate or cut off roamers. Vertical support for site execution.

---

## Execute Sequence

1. **Drone phase** — Ventilation team (Hound/Sarge/Smigs) drones Hallway and Workshop approaches. Upstairs team (Peej/Grant) drones Red Stairs, Long Hallway, and 2F roamer positions.

2. **Roamer pressure (Upstairs team)** — Peej and Grant move upstairs via Red Stairs approach. Establish crosses on 2F roamers. Eliminate or force rotations before main push.

3. **Bathroom push (Ventilation team)** — Hound, Sarge, and Smigs push through Bathroom toward Workshop. Destroy or clear gadgets on approach. Utility denial on key doorways.

4. **Workshop control** — Ventilation team establishes control of Workshop space. Identify bathroom wall for plant approach.

5. **Bathroom wall breach** — Smigs breaches bathroom wall, establishing secondary angle into site or pressure point.

6. **Security Room entry** — Upstairs team (Peej/Grant) pushes into Long Hallway and approaches Security Room from 2F side. Establish control of room.

7. **Coordinate push** — Ventilation team provides lower pressure (Bathroom/Workshop side). Upstairs team provides upper pressure (Long Hallway/Security side). Defenders squeezed from both directions.

8. **Site clear** — Establish full site control. Clear Offices and Security Room. Identify safe plant locations.

9. **Plant** — Plant in Lobby area or deep Workshop position depending on defender positions and remaining utility.

10. **Post-plant** — Team secures perimeter, holds cross angles, denies defuser plays.

---

## Plant Position (PP)

**Primary:** Lobby / Workshop area — central position with cover
**Alternate:** Bathroom extension if defenders are weighted toward Security side

---

## Post-Plant

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Hound** | Bathroom side | Anti-gadget denial; watches Bathroom flank |
| **Sarge** | Site perimeter | Holds cross angles; denies rotations |
| **Smigs** | Site perimeter / corner | Holds secondary angles; supports defense |
| **Peej** | Long Hallway / 2F side | Holds upstairs denial; prevents rotation from 2F |
| **Grant** | Security Room / underneath | Holds defuser challenge angle; prevents late rotation |

---

## Watch For

- **Kapkan at Red Stairs:** Standard roamer trap placement. Upstairs team must drone and clear before committing. Loss of player to trap = loss of vertical advantage.

- **Roamer from Break Room / Admin area:** Secondary defender may push from 2F side during execute. Peej/Grant must establish crosses early before Ventilation team fully commits.

- **Aruni at Security Room entry:** Gate on Security Room doorway will damage team pushing through. Destroy or account for damage.

- **Valkyrie cameras on Red Stairs, Long Hallway, exterior windows:** Look for cams. Destroy early if spotted to deny intel to defenders.

- **Mute disruptors on 2F:** May prevent utility placement or gadget usage. Account for signal jamming.

- **Two hatches from Security Room:** Defense will likely have one operator managing both hatches for vertical denial. Expect roamer from below watching both hatch positions.

- **Bathroom ↔ Tellers wall extension:** Post-rework, Bathroom connects toward Tellers. Don't get flanked from unexpected Tellers-side pressure.

---

## Win Conditions

### Win A — Primary: Dual Pressure Push ✅
**When:** Roamer on 2F is eliminated/denied, Red Stairs is clear, Ventilation approach is open, Smigs utility is ready.

Upstairs team (Peej/Grant) establishes crosses and roamer denial. Ventilation team (Hound/Sarge/Smigs) pushes through Bathroom and Workshop. Site is squeezed from both directions. Plant executes safely.

**Who calls it:** Grant calls "Roamers cleared" from upstairs. Hound confirms Ventilation approach is clear. Sarge initiates coordinate push.

---

### Win B — Ventilation Pressure Only
**When:** Upstairs roamer is strong or well-positioned, Ventilation approach is more open than 2F approach.

Focus pressure on Ventilation/Bathroom side. Upstairs team (Peej/Grant) becomes supportive distraction rather than primary push. Ventilation team (Hound/Sarge/Smigs) executes site from below.

**Who calls it:** Sarge calls "Going Vent-first" early. Peej confirms roamer strong and backs off primary push role.

---

### Win C — Upstairs Chaos / Quick Roamer Win
**When:** Peej/Grant get early roamer kill(s), creating 5v3 or 5v4 early advantage.

Upstairs team pushes harder and faster. Early roamer elimination means Security Room is thin. Coordinate full push immediately.

**Who calls it:** Grant announces roamer kill; team accelerates primary execute.

---

### Win D — Bathroom Wall Denied
**When:** Smigs is dead, hard breach is banned, Bathroom wall has hardened defender (Castle, etc.).

**Adjustment:** Shift primary pressure to upstairs (Red Stairs/Long Hallway approach). Peej and Grant push harder into Security Room. Hound/Sarge hold lower pressure as support or secondary angle. Plant may go in Security Room instead of Workshop.

**Who calls it:** Smigs calls "Breach unavailable, going upstairs-primary."

---

## Audibles

- **Red Stairs locked down:** Don't force Upstairs team into dense trap/defender setup. Fall back to Ventilation-only pressure.
- **Ventilation approach too strong:** Pivot to Red Stairs/upstairs pressure. Abandon Bathroom approach if utility cost is too high.
- **Both upstairs operators down early:** Play 3v5 from Ventilation side. Don't push upstairs again — consolidate lower site pressure.
- **Smigs dies without breaching Bathroom:** Replan plant position. Bathroom breach may not be available — adjust to available angles.

---

## Formation Notes

The 3-2 formation creates asymmetric pressure: Ventilation team (3-man) applies sustained lower pressure, Upstairs team (2-man) applies tactical upper pressure. The key is coordination — if one vector becomes denied, the other team must pivot quickly. Flexibility in plant position (Workshop vs. Security vs. Bathroom area) keeps defenders guessing. Monitor both Bathroom wall and Red Stairs approach throughout — if either fully closes, call the audible immediately.

---
