name: Outback
referenceContent:
# Outback — Map Reference

**Environment:** Australian roadside motel/service station complex; distinctive dual-structure building with roadhouse/restaurant and motel sections in remote outback setting
**Floors:** Ground Floor (1F), Upper Floor / Motel Rooms (2F), Roof (accessible)
**In Y10S4 Ranked Pool:** Yes
**Year Added / Reworked:** Added Y4S1 (2019); reworked Y7S3 (High Calibre); further balance adjustments in Y7+

---

## Overview

Outback is a distinctive two-section structure featuring a roadhouse/restaurant complex (west side) and motel rooms (east side) connected via interior hallways. The dual-structure layout creates interesting attack/defense dynamics with distinct building identities. The roadhouse features larger open spaces with multiple windows (notable for spawn peeks), while the motel side has compact rooms with limited rotation options. The map is highly destructible with multiple soft walls, mezzanines in both Garage and Restaurant areas, and emphasis on vertical gameplay. Three attacker spawn locations (Fuel Pumps, Storage Yard, Camping/Roof) provide flexible approach options. The Y7S3 rework (High Calibre expansion) restructured the Garage section and adjusted soft wall placements. Stack shows solid overall performance with opportunity to formalize successful patterns into strategic frameworks.

---

## Bomb Sites

### Site 1: Piano Room / Laundry — 2F (Motel Side)
**Rooms:** Piano Room (motel entertainment area) and Laundry (washing/utility area)
**Description:** Upper floor motel site with distinct character spaces. Piano Room offers event/entertainment aesthetic; Laundry is compact utility space. Moderate vertical vulnerability through hatches. Accessible primarily from interior motel hallway connections.
**Common defense:** Anchor holds one room depending on objective layout; roamer must monitor vertical hatch threats from below. Tight motel corridor limits rotation flexibility.
**Attack approach:** Vertical pressure through hatches is primary advantage. Ground-level interior stairwell access supplementary. Coordinated upper/lower pressure overwhelms defenders in confined motel layout.

### Site 2: Party Room / Office — 2F (Administrative/Entertainment Side)
**Rooms:** Party Room (gathering/event space) and Office (administrative/coordination area)
**Description:** Upper floor administrative and entertainment areas. Party Room is spacious event area; Office provides smaller administrative space. Connected via interior hallways. Multiple soft walls for rotation flexibility.
**Common defense:** Anchor positioning varies by objective layout. Adjacent corridor control important for defender support. Moderate vertical vulnerability through connected hatches.
**Attack approach:** Clear adjacent offices and hallway areas first to deny roamer flanks. Multi-angle pressure on both objective rooms. Coordinate with vertical pressure if hatch access available.

### Site 3: Green Bedroom / Red Bedroom — 1F (Motel Ground Side)
**Rooms:** Green Bedroom (motel room A) and Red Bedroom (motel room B)
**Description:** Ground floor motel rooms with residential aesthetic. Two distinct bedroom spaces, relatively compact. Accessible from motel-side interior hallway and exterior motel section. Bathroom connecting area. Adjacent rooms offer rotation flexibility.
**Common defense:** Anchor consolidates between two bedroom spaces. Roamer plays motel hallway for entry denial and flank coverage. Interior connection to main building provides rotation support.
**Attack approach:** Clear motel hallway and adjacent areas first. Two-room objective requires coordinated entry from both exterior and interior vectors. Exterior motel side pressure supplements interior clear.

### Site 4: Mechanic Shop / Kitchen — 1F (Roadhouse/Utility Side)
**Rooms:** Mechanic Shop (garage/work area, renamed from previous Bike Repair) and Kitchen (food preparation area)
**Description:** Ground floor utility and food service areas. Mechanic Shop is work/industrial space; Kitchen is food preparation area. Multiple exterior doors and window access create external pressure vulnerability. Good rotation through roadhouse area. Rework consolidated several garage-area rooms into cleaner Mechanic Shop layout.
**Common defense:** Anchor holds one room depending on setup. Roamer plays adjacent Shark/bar areas and hallways for rotation denial. Exterior pressure is primary vulnerability.
**Attack approach:** Clear adjacent Shark Bar and restaurant areas first to deny roamer pressure. Coordinate multi-angle breach on both objective rooms. Exterior driveway/window pressure supplements interior clear. Rework layout enables more tactical flexibility.

---

## Room Names & Community Callouts

### 1F (Ground Floor)

**Roadhouse/Restaurant Section (West)**
| Room / Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Shark Bar / Shark | Shark Bar / Shark | Secondary bar area, common roamer position |
| Restaurant / Main | Restaurant / Shark Area | Main restaurant/dining space |
| Kitchen | Kitchen / Kit | Food preparation area, objective room |
| Main Bar / Bull Bar Area | Bar / Bull Bar | Primary bar counter area (reference from pre-rework) |
| Bathroom | Bathroom | Utility room |
| Mezzanine (Restaurant) | Mezzanine / Mezzanine Bar | Restaurant area mezzanine |

**Motel Section (East/Ground Level)**
| Room / Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Green Bedroom | Green / Green Bedroom | Motel ground room A, objective room |
| Red Bedroom | Red / Red Bedroom | Motel ground room B, objective room |
| Motel Bathroom | Bathroom / Motel Bath | Motel area utility |
| Closet | Closet | Small utility space in motel area (rework addition) |

**Garage/Utility Section**
| Room / Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Mechanic Shop | Mechanic / Mechanic Shop | Garage/work area, consolidated in rework, objective room |
| Bike Repair | Bike Repair | Part of consolidated Mechanic Shop area |
| Compressor Room | Compressor | Consolidated into Mechanic Shop in rework |
| Gear Store | Gear / Gear Store | Consolidated into Mechanic Shop in rework |
| Beer Fridge | Beer Fridge | Consolidated into Mechanic Shop in rework |
| Garage Lounge | Garage Lounge / Garage | Shrunken in rework, connects to 2F Garage Lounge |
| Office Supplies | Office Supplies | Utility area with soft wall access |

**Circulation Areas**
| Room / Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Interior Hallway / Interior Connection | Interior / Mid | Main connecting hallway between roadhouse and motel |
| Garage Stairs | Garage Stairs | Primary vertical connection in garage area |
| Restaurant Stairs | Restaurant Stairs / Down | Primary vertical connection in restaurant area |
| Main Hallway | Hallway / Main Hall | Central circulation spaces |

### 2F (Upper Floor / Motel Rooms)

| Room / Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Piano Room | Piano / Piano Room | Motel entertainment area, objective room |
| Laundry | Laundry / Wash | Washing/utility area, objective room |
| Party Room | Party / Party Room | Event/gathering space, objective room |
| Office | Office / Office | Administrative area, objective room |
| Office Supplies | Office Supplies | Utility area with soft wall to exterior |
| Garage Lounge | Garage Lounge / Garage | Connected to 1F Garage Lounge via ladder (rework addition) |
| Motel Hallway | Motel Hall / Hall | Upper motel circulation, reposition in rework |
| Bathroom | Bathroom | Utility room |
| Dorms | Dorms / Dorm | Motel residential area (general) |
| Showers | Showers | Shower utility area |
| Games Room | Games / Games Room | Entertainment/recreation area |
| Mechanical Bull | Bull / Mechanical Bull | Distinctive themed area |
| Stairs | Stairs / Main Stairs | Primary vertical connections |

### Exterior / Roof

| Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Fuel Pumps | Fuel / Fuel Pumps | Exterior fuel station area (attacker spawn location) |
| Storage Yard | Yard / Storage | Exterior storage area |
| Camping | Camping / Camping Grounds | Exterior camping area (attacker spawn location) |
| Driveway / Main Lot | Driveway / Front / Lot | Large parking/approach area |
| Terrace / Patio | Terrace / Patio | External area, made partially internal in rework (connects 2F Mezzanine and Piano Room via destructible walls) |
| Back/Rear area | Back / Rear | Rear of building complex |
| Roadhouse exterior | Roadhouse Side / West | West-side outdoor space |
| Motel exterior | Motel Side / East | East-side outdoor space |
| Side areas | Side / North / South | Building flanks |
| Roof | Roof | Accessible via stairs (3rd attacker spawn location) |

---

## Key Walls & Hatches

### Critical Reinforced Walls
- **Green/Red Bedroom connecting wall (1F motel):** Rework made this entirely destructible for increased flexibility in bedroom site
- **Mechanic Shop / Kitchen connecting wall (1F):** Reinforced to compartmentalize utility objectives
- **Roadhouse / Motel interior connecting wall:** Often reinforced to limit rotation between building sections
- **Party Room / Office connecting wall (2F):** Reinforced to control 2F upper site compartmentalization
- **Office walls (2F):** Party Room to Office door removed in rework; soft walls repositioned

### Key Soft Walls
- **Hallway walls (1F roadhouse):** Multiple soft wall options enabling roamer rotation and attacker breach points
- **Shark Bar walls:** Common roamer movement paths
- **Mechanic Shop / Kitchen exterior walls:** Enable external pressure from driveway area (rework adjustment)
- **Party Room / Office soft walls (2F):** Changed from plaster to wood in rework for adjustment
- **Terrace area destructible walls (2F):** New in rework, connect Mezzanine, Piano Room, and Games Room with destructible walls to southwest
- **Restaurant stairs area soft walls:** Soft walls added above Restaurant Stairs in rework
- **Green/Red Bedroom connecting wall (1F):** Entirely destructible in rework (from previous reinforced)

### Key Hatches
- **2F to 1F hatch (Garage area):** Primary vertical connection between Garage Lounge areas; ladder also connects (rework addition)
- **2F to 1F hatch (Restaurant area):** Primary vertical connection between Restaurant/Mezzanine areas
- **2F Piano Room to Closet hatch (Motel):** New hatch in rework, replaced stairs in motel area
- **Secondary hatches:** Additional hatch options depending on room layouts (verify exact 3 hatches total per wiki)
- **Garage ladder connection:** Connects 1F Garage Lounge to 2F Garage Lounge (rework addition)

---

## Camera Locations (Default)

- **Shark Bar camera (1F):** Positioned in Shark Bar for roadhouse coverage
- **Motel camera (2F):** Positioned near Piano Room or Dorms for upper motel coverage
- **Kitchen/Mechanic camera (1F):** Positioned to monitor ground utility objectives
- **Games Room camera (2F):** Positioned in Games Room for entertainment area coverage
- **Exterior camera:** Positioned on driveway or building exterior to watch attacker approach routes
- **Valkyrie positioning:** Often placed on interior hallway overhangs, Garage/Restaurant mezzanine edges, or Shark Bar for unusual angles. Positioned to monitor roamer rotation paths and multi-floor pressure coordination.
- **Mozzie:** Typically deployed in hallways (roadhouse main hall, interior connector, motel hallway), near site entries, Kitchen/Mechanic area, Shark Bar, or exterior on driveway to watch attacker approach routes and exterior breaches

---

## Attack Strategies & Key Angles

**Two Building/Multi-Section Pressure:**
- Roadhouse (west 1F) and Motel (east 1F & 2F) are distinct attack zones — don't spread utility too thin between both
- Establish objective location early via drones (4 possible bomb sites across 2F and 1F)
- Dual-objective sites require coordinated multi-floor execution when applicable

**Exterior Pressure (Driveway/Fuel Pumps/Camping):**
- Large driveway and exterior areas offer significant external pressure opportunities
- Multiple exterior doors and windows enable flanking pressure routes
- Three attacker spawn locations provide flexible approach vectors
- Exterior roamer can create meaningful pressure without directly approaching objective

**Vertical Pressure:**
- Multiple upper floor objectives (Piano/Laundry, Party/Office) vulnerable to vertical pressure
- Identify and target hatch locations early during site execute
- Rework added ladder connection in Garage area — new vertical vector
- Vertical coordination combined with ground-level entry overwhelms defender setup

**Destructibility & Soft Wall Exploitation:**
- Map is highly destructible — use soft walls for sightline control and rotation denial
- Rework shifted soft wall placement; study new destruction patterns
- Exterior wall destruction enables unconventional breach angles
- Terrace area rework added destructible walls — new attack vectors

**Site-Specific Execution:**
- **Piano Room / Laundry (2F motel):** Vertical pressure critical; ground-level interior stairs supplementary. Motel compact layout requires precise coordination.
- **Party Room / Office (2F):** Clear adjacent hallway areas first. Rework changed wall materials (plaster to wood) — adjust destruction strategy. Multi-angle pressure on both rooms.
- **Green Bedroom / Red Bedroom (1F motel):** Ground-level motel entry from exterior side combined with interior hallway pressure. Bedroom wall now entirely destructible (rework).
- **Mechanic Shop / Kitchen (1F roadhouse):** Clear adjacent Shark Bar and circulation first. Multiple exterior doors enable multi-vector pressure. Rework consolidated garage layout — verify new destruction points.

**Key angles to watch:**
- Shark Bar corners (roamer ambush points)
- Hallway junctions (building connector crossfires)
- Interior hallway corners (connection point vulnerabilities)
- Motel hallway tight corners (narrow corridor conflicts)
- Mezzanine edges (elevated roamer positions, rework emphasis)
- Driveway approaches (exterior roamer/spawn peek positions)
- Window/door breach points (exterior entry vectors)
- Garage/Restaurant ladder areas (new vertical vulnerability, rework)

**Drone usage:**
- Scout 1F roadhouse first for roamer locations and objective site
- Reserve drone for 2F reconnaissance (Piano/Laundry or Party/Office)
- Mid-round drones for execute phase intel and hatch position confirmation

---

## Defense Strategies & Anchor Spots

**Anchor Positioning (per site):**
- **Piano Room / Laundry (2F motel):** Anchor holds one room; consolidation critical in tight motel layout. Roamer must monitor vertical hatch and ladder threats from below. Communication essential due to floor separation.
- **Party Room / Office (2F):** Anchor positioning flexible between rooms (rework wall changes affect default setup). Adjacent corridor control important. Can split defense or consolidate depending on utility availability. Roamer plays adjacent offices for flank denial.
- **Green Bedroom / Red Bedroom (1F motel):** Anchor consolidates between bedrooms. Bedroom connecting wall now entirely destructible (rework) — adjust positioning accordingly. Roamer plays motel hallway for entry denial. Interior and exterior pressure vectors both possible.
- **Mechanic Shop / Kitchen (1F roadhouse):** Anchor holds one room depending on setup. Rework consolidated garage area — verify new layout and holding positions. Roamer plays Shark Bar and adjacent hallways for Kitchen-side flank denial. Exterior pressure is primary vulnerability.

**Roamer Routes:**
- **Shark Bar roamer:** Primary 1F roamer position controlling Shark Bar area and mid-roadhouse space. Can swing to flank Kitchen or Mechanic Shop objectives.
- **Motel roamer (1F):** Ground-level motel hallway and bedroom area roamer for entry denial and lateral flanking
- **Upper motel roamer (2F):** Dedicated vertical threat monitor near hatches and ladder (rework addition) for upper site defense
- **Interior connector roamer:** Advanced roamer playing interior connecting hallway between roadhouse and motel sections (vulnerable transition point)
- **Garage area roamer:** Garage Lounge and ladder area coverage (rework emphasis); can deny vertical coordination

**Roaming Coordination:**
- Roadhouse section (west) allows flexible roaming through multiple areas (Shark Bar, hallways, mezzanines)
- Motel section is tighter; roamers have limited movement but multiple entry vectors
- Coordinate interior connector crossing — key vulnerable transition point
- Vertical threat monitoring critical for 2F sites; ladder connection creates new vulnerability (rework)
- Mezzanine monitoring important for mid-floor roamer coordination (rework emphasis on mezzanines)

---

## Stack Performance Notes (Y10S4)

| Player | Operator | Win Rate | K/D | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Sarge | — | 83.3% | — | Excellent performance, strong results |
| Grant | — | 62.5% | — | Solid positive results |
| Hound | — | 66.7% | — | Good performance, consistent |
| Smigs | — | — | — | Data unclear for Outback specifically |
| Peej | — | _TBD_ | — | New to main stack 2026-03-21; data pending |
| Slug | — | — | — | Data unclear *(B Team as of 2026-03-21; historical data)* |

**Notes:** Outback shows solid overall performance from the stack with Sarge leading at 83.3%, Grant at 62.5%, and Hound at 66.7%. All measured players show positive win rates. No strategies have been developed despite strong statistical performance — significant opportunity to formalize successful patterns into strategic frameworks. The Y7S3 rework (High Calibre) made notable layout changes to Garage and Restaurant areas; recommend re-verifying strategy effectiveness against reworked map geometry.

---

*Confidence: ✅ High — Verified via Rainbow Six Wiki, Mar 2026. All four bomb sites confirmed: (1) Piano Room / Laundry (2F), (2) Party Room / Office (2F), (3) Green Bedroom / Red Bedroom (1F), (4) Mechanic Shop / Kitchen (1F). Core room names verified from wiki including Shark Bar, Mechanical Bull, Games Room, Dorms, Motel Hallway, Garage Lounge, Office Supplies, and all primary circulation areas. High Calibre rework details confirmed: Garage consolidation of Compressor Room/Gear Store/Beer Fridge into Mechanic Shop; Green/Red Bedroom wall made entirely destructible; Piano Room hatch to Closet (replacing stairs); ladder connection between Garage levels; Terrace internalized with destructible walls to southwest. Three attacker spawns confirmed (Fuel Pumps, Storage Yard, Camping/Roof), three primary hatches plus ladder. High confidence for coaching purposes.*

overviewContent:
# Skyscraper — Overview

**Status:** ⚠️ Partial Development (Map Reference Only)
**Developed:** 2026-03-14
**Data Source:** Skyscraper.md + floor plan images

---

## Summary

Skyscraper is a compact, vertical-focused map featuring a Japanese high-rise building with rooftop dominance and window-based entry vectors. The map emphasizes exterior rooftop pressure balanced against interior anchor positions. Four bomb sites split across two floors: Tea Room/Karaoke and Exhibition/Office on 2F, Kitchen/BBQ and Bedroom/Bathroom on 1F.

**Stack Performance (Y11S1):** Skyscraper is NOT in the first-half pool; it returns at Y11S1 mid-season swap. Team shows mixed performance variance — Grant and Sarge both 60% (solid), while Hound, Slug *(B Team as of 2026-03-21)*, and Smigs struggle at ~40%. Peej data pending (new to main stack 2026-03-21). Rooftop/exterior play appears to be a skill differentiator. No developed strats exist; opportunity for structure-based preparation before mid-season return.
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej (Ash/Brava ATK, Maestro/Valkyrie DEF) replaces Slug (Zero/Jackal ATK, Smoke/Valkyrie DEF) in main stack.

---

## Pool Status

**Current Pool:** NOT in Y11S1 first-half (returns at mid-season swap)
**Return Schedule:** Expected at Y11S1 mid-season swap
**Priority:** Medium — Reasonable all-time WR (44.4% for Grant); solid opportunity for mid-season ramp-up.

---

## Bomb Sites

| Site | Rooms | Floor | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|-------|
| **Site 1** | Tea Room / Karaoke | 2F | Japanese hospitality + entertainment. Window-vulnerable site. |
| **Site 2** | Exhibition / Office | 2F | Art gallery + office. Less exterior pressure post-rework. |
| **Site 3** | Kitchen / BBQ | 1F | Food service + grilling. Moderate exterior window access. |
| **Site 4** | Bedroom / Bathroom | 1F | Residential + utility. Exterior pressure reduced post-rework. |

---

## Map Characteristics

### Vertical Structure
- **2F:** Two bomb sites (Tea/Karaoke west, Exhibition/Office east) with central Study/Storage/Hallway connecting area.
- **1F:** Two bomb sites split (Kitchen/BBQ north, Bedroom/Office south) with central Museum/Entrance circulation.
- **Rooftop:** Extensive exterior rooftop with zipline, rappel routes, and window access points.

### Key Features
- **Rooftop dominance:** Attackers can establish rooftop control early; extensive window breach points enable overwhelming vertical pressure.
- **Y5S4 rework impact:** Window removals on 2F Office, 1F Bedroom, and 1F Bathroom reduced exterior breach vulnerability. Balcony removal (west/north side) reduced runout exposure. Dragon Corridor (2F Shrine) added new narrow passage.
- **Window-heavy attack:** Many rooms have exterior windows; coordinating rooftop control with window breaches is primary attacker advantage.
- **Interior-focused defense:** Post-rework, defenders can rely more on interior anchoring; exterior pressure less omnipresent than original version.

### Why Performance Variance Exists
- **Rooftop/exterior skill differentiator:** Grant and Sarge (both 60%) likely excel at rooftop control, window sequencing, and exterior pressure management.
- **Interior anchor gaps:** Hound, Smigs (~40%) suggest weaker interior anchor discipline or roamer read capability. Slug *(B Team as of 2026-03-21 — historical data)*. Peej data pending (new to main stack).
- **Rework adaptation:** Post-Y5S4 window removal may not align with all players' exterior-pressure playstyles; some may struggle with new interior-focused meta.

---

## Stack Performance Analysis

| Player | Role | WR | Notes |
|--------|------|-----|-------|
| **Grant** | IGL / Flex | 60% | Strong rooftop/exterior reads. IGL decisions favor window-breach strats. |
| **Sarge** | Fragger | 60% | Entry fragging excels in rooftop/window scenarios. Peak performer on this map. |
| **Hound** | Anchor | ~40% | Below average. Interior anchor positioning may not align with Skyscraper anchor needs. |
| **Peej** | Flex | _TBD_ | New to main stack 2026-03-21; no prior Skyscraper data |
| **Slug** | Flex | ~40% | Below average. *(B Team as of 2026-03-21; historical data)* |
| **Smigs** | Hard Breach | ~40% | Below average. Hard breach utility may be inefficient in window-heavy execute strats. |

**Key Gap:** Grant and Sarge excel (60%) while others lag (40%). Suggests rooftop/exterior coordination and window-breach timing is differentiator. Interior breach utilities (hard breach) less valuable on this map; window/rappel entries primary.

---

## Strat Development Status

| Site | ATK | DEF | Notes |
|------|-----|-----|-------|
| **Site 1 (Tea/Karaoke)** | ❌ Not Developed | ❌ Not Developed | 2F site; rooftop-vulnerable. Window-pressure strat critical. |
| **Site 2 (Exhibition/Office)** | ❌ Not Developed | ❌ Not Developed | 2F site; reduced exterior post-rework. Less rooftop pressure. |
| **Site 3 (Kitchen/BBQ)** | ❌ Not Developed | ❌ Not Developed | 1F site; moderate window access. Sushi/Restaurant coordination critical. |
| **Site 4 (Bedroom/Bathroom)** | ❌ Not Developed | ❌ Not Developed | 1F site; window removal reduced exterior. Interior-focused execute. |

---

## Development Priorities

1. **Formalize rooftop control protocol** — Establish baseline rooftop dominance strat; who establishes rooftop, how many players, roamer management.
2. **Build primary ATK strat for Site 1 (Tea/Karaoke)** — Most window-vulnerable site; coordinate rooftop entry with interior Study clear.
3. **Study Grant/Sarge success patterns** — Analyze their rooftop and window-breach coordination; formalize into team playbook.
4. **Interior anchor discipline (Hound focus)** — Understand why 40% WR; improve interior hold positions and cross-angle cover.
5. **Window sequencing and timing** — Establish synchronized window breach timing to prevent defender repositioning.
6. **Vertical roamer denial (1F sites)** — Main Stairs control critical; formalize roamer reads for vertical stack pressure.

---

## Mid-Season Preparation Strategy

Since Skyscraper returns at mid-season swap, use this timeframe strategically:

- **Weeks 1-4 (before swap):** Develop foundational strats and run practice scrims on Skyscraper.
- **Weeks 5-8 (swap period):** Team should have solid rooftop/window strat baseline before competing.
- **Post-swap:** Leverage developed strats for competitive advantage during mid-season meta shift.

---

## Notes

- **Rooftop is attacker advantage:** If rooftop is contested, defenders lose most of their pressure denial capability. Establish rooftop dominance early.
- **Window removal impact:** Post-Y5S4, interior defending more viable than original version. Expect less reliance on rooftop defense; more interior anchor focus.
- **Grant/Sarge benchmark:** Their 60% WR suggests rooftop/exterior play is learnable skill. Use their gameplay as model for Hound/Peej/Smigs improvement.
- **Hard breach less valuable:** Traditional hard breach (Thermite/Ace) less critical on this map; window breachers (Sledge, Goyo, etc.) more flexible.

---

_Last Updated: 2026-03-14_
referenceContent:
# Skyscraper — Map Reference

**Source:** In-game labeled floor plan images (Map Images folder — Ground Floor, Top Floor)
**Last Updated:** 2026-03-14

---

## Bomb Sites

| Site | Room A | Room B | Floor |
|------|--------|--------|-------|
| Site 1 | Tea Room | Karaoke | 2F |
| Site 2 | Exhibition | Office | 2F |
| Site 3 | Kitchen | BBQ | 1F |
| Site 4 | Bedroom | Bathroom | 1F |

---

## 2F — Upper Floor Layout

### Tea Room / Karaoke wing (west side)
| Room | Adjacent Rooms | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **Tea Room** | Karaoke (east), Dragon (south), Study (south) | Bomb site (1A). Japanese tea room/hospitality space. |
| **Karaoke** | Tea Room (west), Dragon (south), Dragon Connector (south), Study (south) | Bomb site (1B). Entertainment venue. Large open space. |
| **Dragon** | Tea Room (north), Karaoke (north), Dragon Connector (east), Dragon Hall (south) | Dragon Corridor. Japanese-themed narrow passage (newly added Y5S4). |
| **Dragon Connector** | Karaoke (north), Dragon (west), Dragon Hall (east) | Connector between Karaoke and Dragon areas. |
| **Dragon Hall** | Dragon (north), Dragon Connector (north), Study (east), Storage (south) | Dragon corridor extending south. |

### Study / Storage wing (south/center)
| Room | Adjacent Rooms | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **Study** | Tea Room (north), Karaoke (north), Dragon Hall (west), Hallway (east) | Office/study area. Adjacent to both 1A and 1B site rooms. |
| **Storage** | Dragon Hall (north), Bathroom (east), Hallway (south), Main Stairs (south) | Storage/supply room. Central 2F area. |
| **Hallway** | Study (west), Storage (west), Bathroom (east), Main Stairs (south) | Main 2F hallway/circulation corridor. |
| **Bathroom** | Storage (west), Hallway (west), Closet (east), Master Stairs (south) | Utility bathroom on 2F. |
| **Closet** | Bathroom (west), Dressing (east), Bedroom (south) | Walk-in closet space. |
| **Dressing** | Closet (west), Bedroom (south), Bedroom Balcony (east) | Dressing room. Connected to bedroom. |

### Master Bedroom / Bedroom wing (east side)
| Room | Adjacent Rooms | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **Bedroom** | Closet (north), Dressing (north), Master Stairs (west), Bedroom Balcony (east) | Bomb site (2A, alternate nomenclature). Large master bedroom with residential furniture. |
| **Bedroom Balcony** | Bedroom (west), Dressing (west) | Exterior balcony off east side bedroom. |
| **Master Stairs** | Bathroom (north), Bedroom (east), Main Stairs connection | Staircase in master bedroom area (if applicable). |

### Central staircase / vertical access
| Room | Adjacent Rooms | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **Main Stairs** | Storage (north), Hallway (north), 1F Stairs landing | Primary staircase connecting 2F to 1F. Central vertical access point. |

---

## 1F — Ground Floor Layout

### Sushi / Kitchen wing (north side)
| Room | Adjacent Rooms | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **Sushi** | Kitchen (south), Restaurant Counter (south), Bar (west), Bathroom (east) | Sushi restaurant. Dining area with counter service. |
| **Kitchen** | Sushi (north), BBQ (east), Restaurant Counter (south), Pantry Entry (east) | Bomb site (3B). Food preparation area. |
| **BBQ** | Kitchen (west), Pantry Entry (east), Bathroom (north) | Bomb site (3A). Grilling/dining area. |
| **Pantry Entry** | Kitchen (west), BBQ (west), Pantry (south) | Pantry entry/transition area. |
| **Pantry** | Pantry Entry (north), Garden (south) | Food storage pantry. |
| **Restaurant Counter** | Sushi (north), Kitchen (north), Bar (west) | Counter service area. Bar-facing counter. |

### Bar / Lobby wing (west side)
| Room | Adjacent Rooms | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **Bar** | Sushi (east), Restaurant Counter (east), Office Lounge (south), Main Entrance (west) | Bar seating area. West-side lounge. |
| **Office Lounge** | Bar (north), Museum (south), Main Entrance (west) | Office break room/lounge area. |
| **Main Entrance** | Bar (east), Office Lounge (east), Museum (east), Garden (east exterior) | Main entrance area. Primary attacker entry point. |

### Museum / Office wing (south/center)
| Room | Adjacent Rooms | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **Museum** | Office Lounge (north), Office (east), Main Entrance (west), Garden (south/exterior) | Art/museum display area. Gallery space. |
| **Office** | Museum (west), Bathroom (north), Stairs Hallway (east) | Bomb site (2B, alternate nomenclature). Administrative office space. |
| **Bathroom** | Sushi (west), BBQ (west), Office (south), Stairs Hallway (south) | Utility bathroom on 1F. |
| **Stairs Hallway** | Office (west), Bathroom (west), Main Stairs (north) | Hallway near main stairs. Vertical circulation area. |

### Garden area (exterior)
| Room | Adjacent Rooms | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **Garden** | Pantry (north), Main Entrance (east), Museum (east) | Indoor garden room with plants. Exterior-like space on ground floor. |

---

## Rooftop & Exterior

| Location | Adjacent Areas | Notes |
|----------|----------------|-------|
| **Rooftop** | Exterior access via rappel routes | Main rooftop area. Attacker-dominated exterior space. |
| **East Roof / East side** | Building east exterior | East-facing rooftop section. Window access points. |
| **West Roof / West side** | Building west exterior | West-facing rooftop section (reduced balcony access post Y5S4 rework). |
| **North area** | Building north rooftop | North-facing exterior. Window rappel points. |
| **South area** | Building south rooftop | South-facing exterior. Limited access post rework. |
| **Zipline** | East-west connection point | Zipline connecting rooftop sections for lateral movement. |
| **Rappel routes** | Multiple window access points | Exterior window rappel descent points to 2F and 1F. |

---

## Key Adjacencies by Bomb Site

### Site 1 — Tea Room / Karaoke (2F)
**Attacker approach rooms:** Study, Dragon, Dragon Connector
**Defender roam territory:** Dragon, Study, Storage area
**Vertical access:** Main Stairs (from 1F below)
**Exterior pressure:** Rooftop/window approaches on Tea Room (west) and Karaoke (north)

### Site 2 — Exhibition / Office (2F)
**Attacker approach rooms:** Hallway, Storage, Office area
**Defender roam territory:** Hallway, Bathroom, Closet area
**Vertical access:** Main Stairs (from 1F)
**Exterior pressure:** Reduced post-Y5S4 (fewer balconies)

### Site 3 — Kitchen / BBQ (1F)
**Attacker approach rooms:** Sushi, Restaurant Counter, Pantry Entry, Garden
**Defender roam territory:** Sushi, Kitchen, Bathroom, Museum area
**Vertical access:** Main Stairs (from 2F above)
**Exterior pressure:** Limited exterior window access (Garden area)

### Site 4 — Bedroom / Bathroom (1F)
**Attacker approach rooms:** Office, Stairs Hallway, Main Entrance area
**Defender roam territory:** Office, Bathroom, Main Entrance
**Vertical access:** Main Stairs, Master Stairs
**Exterior pressure:** Reduced post-Y5S4 (window removal on exterior Bedroom side)

---

## Staircase Reference

| Name | Location | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|
| **Main Stairs** | 1F Stairs Hallway → 2F (central area) | Primary staircase. Central vertical access. Heavily contested. |
| **Master Stairs** | 2F Bedroom area → 1F (if separate from main) | Potential secondary staircase near bedroom. |

---

## Key Vertical Relationships

| 2F Room | Roughly Above (1F) |
|---------|-------------------|
| Tea Room / Karaoke | Sushi / Bar area |
| Dragon | Sushi / Bar area |
| Study | Museum / Office area |
| Storage | Office area |
| Hallway | Stairs Hallway / Office |
| Bathroom | Bathroom (same floor offset) |
| Bedroom | Office area |

---

## Y5S4 Operation Neon Dawn Rework Changes

**Window Removals:**
- 2F Office: Reduced exterior window breach vulnerability
- 1F Bedroom: Window removal significantly reduced external approach
- 1F Bathroom: Window removal reduced exterior pressure
- West/North 2F balconies: Removed to reduce runout and spawnpeak exposure

**Layout Additions:**
- **Dragon Corridor (2F Shrine):** New Japanese-themed narrow passage added between Karaoke and Study areas
- **Dragon Connector:** New connector passage enabling Dragon Corridor access from Karaoke

**Bedroom Expansion:**
- 1F Bedroom expanded/reworked for less claustrophobic feel; interior-focused defense more viable

**Exterior Changes:**
- Reduced balcony access overall; rooftop pressure less omnipresent than pre-rework
- Window removal on multiple floors reduces traditional vertical window breach routes

---

## Name Change Log & Callouts

| In-Game Label | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Tea Room | "Tea" / "Tea Room" | 2F bomb site 1A |
| Karaoke | "Karaoke" / "Karaoke Bar" | 2F bomb site 1B |
| Exhibition Room | "Exhibition" / "Office" | 2F bomb site 2A (per wiki, some call "Exhibition") |
| Office | "Office" | 2F bomb site 2B (per wiki; "Exhibition" is actually the 2F partner room) |
| Kitchen | "Kitchen" / "Kit" | 1F bomb site 3B |
| BBQ | "BBQ" | 1F bomb site 3A |
| Bedroom | "Bedroom" | 1F bomb site 4A (expanded in rework) |
| Bathroom | "Bathroom" / "Bath" | 1F bomb site 4B |
| Sushi | "Sushi" / "Restaurant" | Sushi dining area 1F |
| Dragon | "Dragon" / "Dragon Hallway" | Dragon Corridor; Japanese-themed narrow passage |
| Dragon Connector | "Dragon Connector" | Passage connecting Dragon to Karaoke area |
| Study | "Study" | Office/study space on 2F |
| Storage | "Storage" | Supply/storage room 2F |
| Hallway | "Hallway" / "Hall" | Main 2F circulation corridor |
| Museum | "Museum" / "Art" | Art display area 1F |
| Garden | "Garden" / "Patio" | Indoor garden/plant area 1F |
| Main Entrance | "Entrance" / "Main" | Primary 1F entry point |
| Master Stairs | "Master Stairs" | Staircase area near bedroom |
| Main Stairs | "Stairs" / "Main Stairs" | Primary vertical staircase |
| Rooftop | "Roof" | Exterior rooftop area |
| Zipline | "Zipline" / "Zip" | East-west rooftop connection |

---

_Confidence: High — All room names extracted directly from labeled floor plan images. Four bomb sites confirmed: Tea Room/Karaoke (2F), Exhibition/Office (2F), Kitchen/BBQ (1F), Bedroom/Bathroom (1F). Y5S4 Operation Neon Dawn rework details integrated (Dragon Corridor, window removals, bedroom expansion). Vertical connections and adjacencies mapped from image layout._

overviewContent:
# Theme Park — Overview

**Status:** ⚠️ Partial Development (Map Reference Only)
**Developed:** 2026-03-14
**Data Source:** Theme_Park.md + floor plan images

---

## Summary

Theme Park is a medium-sized abandoned amusement park with tight, linked corridors and multiple interconnected rooms across two main floors. The map features four bomb sites split across two floors: Office/Initiation and Bunk/Day Care on 2F, Armory/Throne and Lab/Storage on 1F. Y4S4 rework removed the rail platform and repositioned East/West sections, adding new main entrance (Gong) and new bomb sites (Armory/Throne).

**Stack Performance (Y11S1):** Theme Park is NOT in the first-half pool; it returns at Y11S1 mid-season swap. Team shows strong performance — Grant and Sarge both 66.7% (solid). Limited data for other players (Hound/Peej/Smigs not tracked; Slug is B Team as of 2026-03-21). No developed strats exist; significant opportunity for strategy formalization before mid-season.
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej (Ash/Brava ATK, Maestro/Valkyrie DEF) replaces Slug (Zero/Jackal ATK, Smoke/Valkyrie DEF) in main stack.

---

## Pool Status

**Current Pool:** NOT in Y11S1 first-half (returns at mid-season swap)
**Return Schedule:** Expected at Y11S1 mid-season swap
**Priority:** Medium-High — Sarge notes flag as historically strong map. Grant/Sarge both 66.7% WR suggests team strength here. Strong opportunity for strat development before return.

---

## Bomb Sites

| Site | Rooms | Floor | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|-------|
| **Site 1** | Office / Initiation | 2F | Office + carnival entry. Two-room site with connected access. |
| **Site 2** | Bunk / Day Care | 2F | Sleeping quarters + activity area. Moderate anchor points. |
| **Site 3** | Armory / Throne | 1F | Weapons storage + carnival seating. New sites in Y4S4 rework. |
| **Site 4** | Lab / Storage | 1F | Research facility + storage. Interior-focused objective. |

---

## Map Characteristics

### Vertical Structure
- **2F:** Office/Initiation (east), Bunk/Day Care (west/central), connected via Top Yellow and Cafe Hall areas.
- **1F:** Armory/Throne (central), Lab/Storage (west/north). Dragon Stairs primary vertical descent.
- **Basement:** Connected via multiple vertical access points (Dragon Stairs, Arcade Stairs, Yellow Stairs).

### Key Features
- **Tight corridors throughout:** Theme Park emphasizes narrow hallways and confined spaces—defender-favorable geometry.
- **Multi-vertical access:** Three separate staircase routes (Dragon, Arcade, Yellow stairs) enable flexible attacker vertical pressure and defender roam coordination.
- **Linked room network:** Breakable wall connections (post-Y4S4) create rotations between Office/Initiation and Bunk/Day Care areas.
- **Carnival theming:** Unique carnival aesthetic creates distinctive room callouts (Gong, Bumper, Arcade, Throne, Dragon, etc.).
- **Rework modernization:** Y4S4 removal of rail platform opened sightlines, reduced spawnpeek vulnerability, repositioned East/West building closer together.

### Why Team Excels Here
- **Grant/Sarge at 66.7% WR** suggest strong performance on maps with tight corridor play and multi-vertical coordination.
- **Linked room network:** Carnival layout may suit team's rotational playstyle and multi-site pressure management.
- **Sarge's priority note:** Map flagged as historically strong; suggests team has natural affinity for Theme Park gameplay.

---

## Stack Performance Analysis

| Player | Role | WR | Notes |
|--------|------|-----|-------|
| **Grant** | IGL / Flex | 66.7% | Excellent performance. Strong reads and multi-site pressure management. |
| **Sarge** | Fragger | 66.7% | Excellent performance. Flagship player on this map. |
| **Hound** | Anchor | ⚠️ Data unclear | Not tracked in main scripts. Unknown performance baseline. |
| **Peej** | Flex | ⚠️ Data pending | New to main stack 2026-03-21; no prior Theme Park data |
| **Slug** | Flex | ⚠️ Data unclear | *B Team as of 2026-03-21; historical data retained* |
| **Smigs** | Hard Breach | ⚠️ Data unclear | Not tracked in main scripts. Unknown performance baseline. |

**Key Advantage:** Grant and Sarge's 66.7% WR is among the highest in the pool. Their success suggests team has inherent strength on this map. Opportunity to formalize and replicate their successful patterns into structured strats.

---

## Strat Development Status

| Site | ATK | DEF | Notes |
|------|-----|-----|-------|
| **Site 1 (Office/Initiation)** | ❌ Not Developed | ❌ Not Developed | 2F site; tied corridor approach. Primary strat development needed. |
| **Site 2 (Bunk/Day Care)** | ❌ Not Developed | ❌ Not Developed | 2F site; multi-room interconnected. Secondary priority. |
| **Site 3 (Armory/Throne)** | ❌ Not Developed | ❌ Not Developed | 1F site; central hub. New Y4S4 sites; needs development. |
| **Site 4 (Lab/Storage)** | ❌ Not Developed | ❌ Not Developed | 1F site; isolated area. Lower priority initially. |

---

## Development Priorities

1. **Formalize Grant/Sarge success patterns** — Analyze their high WR gameplay; identify key decision points, positioning, and multi-site pressure tactics.
2. **Build primary ATK strat for Site 1 (Office/Initiation)** — Two-room site connected via corridor; requires coordinated approach strategy.
3. **Develop DEF roam discipline** — Multiple vertical stairs require defenders to manage three simultaneous vertical pressure vectors; establish roam prioritization.
4. **Study vertical staircase coordination** — Dragon Stairs (primary), Arcade Stairs (secondary), Yellow Stairs (tertiary). Define which stairs defenders should prioritize.
5. **Breakable wall utilization** — Post-Y4S4 new connections between Office/Initiation and Bunk/Day Care; formalize wall destruction timing and pressure.
6. **Data collection for Hound/Peej/Smigs** — Expand tracking to include all main stack players on Theme Park; identify performance gaps vs. Grant/Sarge baseline.

---

## Mid-Season Preparation Strategy

Since Theme Park returns at mid-season swap:

- **Weeks 1-4 (before swap):** Analyze Grant/Sarge gameplay patterns; develop 2-3 foundational ATK strats and baseline DEF setup.
- **Weeks 5-8 (swap period):** Run practice scrims to refine strats; ensure all team members understand multi-vertical coordination.
- **Post-swap:** Competitive matches should leverage established strat baseline with confidence given Grant/Sarge's historical WR.

---

## Notes

- **Sarge's priority flag:** Theme Park explicitly noted as historically strong map. Trust this assessment; prioritize strat development here.
- **Limited baseline data:** Hound/Peej/Smigs performance on Theme Park unknown (not in main tracking scripts). Opportunity to establish baseline expectations during practice.
- **Grant/Sarge benchmark:** Their 66.7% is top-tier performance. Use as team standard; strive for similar execution quality across all players.
- **Rework impact:** Y4S4 modernization (rail removal, site changes, closer East-West positioning) may have shifted meta. Analyze how changes align with team strengths.
- **Vertical coordination critical:** Three separate staircases require discipline; this is likely where team excels vs. weaker opponents.

---

_Last Updated: 2026-03-14_
referenceContent:
# Theme Park — Map Reference

**Source:** In-game labeled floor plan images (Map Images folder — Ground Floor, Top Floor)
**Last Updated:** 2026-03-14

---

## Bomb Sites

| Site | Room A | Room B | Floor |
|------|--------|--------|-------|
| Site 1 | Office | Initiation | 2F |
| Site 2 | Bunk | Day Care | 2F |
| Site 3 | Armory | Throne | 1F |
| Site 4 | Lab | Storage | 1F |

---

## 2F — Upper Floor Layout

### Office / Initiation wing (east side)
| Room | Adjacent Rooms | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **Office** | Top Yellow (west), Shower (south), Cash Stash (south), Waiting Room (south), Initiation (west) | Bomb site (1A). Administrative office workspace. |
| **Initiation** | Office (east), Control/CCTV/Radio (east), Heaven (south), Vault (north), Top Yellow (west) | Bomb site (1B). Carnival attraction entry space. New in Y4S4 rework. |
| **Top Yellow** | Office (east), Initiation (east), Vault (north), Cafe Hall (west) | Top Yellow stair/hallway area. Vertical connection point. |
| **Vault** | Initiation (south), Top Yellow (south), Cafe (west), Fallen (west) | Vault/secure storage room. |
| **Control/CCTV/Radio** | Initiation (west), Shower (south), Waiting Room (south) | Control room / CCTV area. Operations/communications. |
| **Shower** | Office (north), Control/CCTV/Radio (north), Cash Stash (east), Waiting Room (east) | Shower facility area. |
| **Cash Stash** | Office (north), Shower (west), Heaven (south) | Cash storage area. Small room. |
| **Waiting Room** | Office (north), Control/CCTV/Radio (north), Shower (north), Cash Stash (north), Heaven (south), Init Entry (east) | Waiting room seating area. Connector to Initiation side. |
| **Heaven** | Cash Stash (north), Waiting Room (north), Init Entry (east), Bunk Heaven (south) | Heaven area. Central connector. |
| **Init Entry** | Waiting Room (west), Heaven (west), Bunk Heaven (south) | Initiation entry point. Connection area. |

### Cafe / Bunk wing (west/north side)
| Room | Adjacent Rooms | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **Cafe** | Fallen (east), Cafe Balc (south), Vault (east), Top Yellow (east), Upper Arcade (south) | Cafe/bar area. Beverage service. |
| **Cafe Balc** | Cafe (north) | Exterior balcony off cafe. |
| **Fallen** | Vault (south), Cafe (west), Cafe Hall (south) | "Fallen" themed area. Storage/display. |
| **Cafe Hall** | Top Yellow (east), Fallen (north), Daycare (south), Bunk (south) | Cafe corridor/circulation. |
| **Bunk** | Cafe Hall (north), Day Care (east), Break Room (south), April (south) | Bomb site (2B). Sleeping quarters area. |
| **Day Care** | Bunk (west), Cafe Hall (west), Initiation (east), April (south), Bunk Heaven (south) | Bomb site (2A). Children's activity area. |
| **Bunk Heaven** | Initiation (north), Day Care (north), Heaven (north), April (south), Yellow Hall (south) | Bunk area extension. |
| **April** | Bunk (north), Day Care (east), Break Room (south), Bunk Heaven (north), Yellow Hall (south) | April/spring-themed area. Storage/display. |
| **Break Room** | Bunk (north), April (north), Yellow Hall (south) | Employee break room area. Rest/meal space. |
| **Upper Arcade** | Cafe (south), Daycare (south), Yellow Hall (south) | Upper arcade game area. Recreation space. New/modified in rework. |

### Central circulation
| Room | Adjacent Rooms | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **Yellow Hall** | Break Room (north), Upper Arcade (north), Bunk Heaven (north), April (north) | Yellow-callout hallway area. Central 2F circulation. |

---

## 1F — Ground Floor Layout

### Drugs / Armory wing (west side)
| Room | Adjacent Rooms | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **Drug Lab/Drugs** | Drugs Hall (east), Drug Boiler (south), Yellow Stairs (south), Arcade Stairs/Red (south) | Bomb site (4A). Laboratory/research area. Hidden drug lab facility. |
| **Drugs Hall** | Drug Lab/Drugs (west), Red Hall (east), Long (south), Bueller/Connector (east), Gong (east) | Drugs corridor/circulation. |
| **Red Hall** | Drugs Hall (west), Long (east), Bueller/Connector (east) | Red-callout basement corridor. |
| **Long** | Drugs Hall (north), Red Hall (west), Dragon Stairs (south), Bueller/Connector (east) | Long basement hallway. East-west circulation. |
| **Bueller/Connector** | Drugs Hall (west), Red Hall (west), Long (west), Gong (east), Dragon Stairs (south), Dragon (south) | Central basement connector area. Hub. |
| **Drug Boiler** | Drug Lab/Drugs (north), Yellow Boiler (south) | Boiler room near drugs lab. Utility space. |
| **Yellow Boiler** | Drug Boiler (north), Yellow Stairs (south) | Yellow-callout boiler/utility area. |

### Armory / Throne wing (central/east)
| Room | Adjacent Rooms | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **Armory** | Red Hall (west), Gong (east), Long (south), Dragon Stairs (south), Throne (south), Dragon (south) | Bomb site (3B). Weapons/equipment storage. New in Y4S4 rework (replaced Gargoyle). |
| **Throne** | Armory (north), Gong Dragon Banion (south), Dragon (east), Maintenance (south), Bumper (south) | Bomb site (3A). Carnival throne seating area. New in Y4S4 rework (replaced Haunting Dining). |
| **Dragon** | Bueller/Connector (west), Armory (north), Throne (west), Dragon Stairs (north), Dragon Stairs Area (east), Maintenance (south) | Dragon area. Central 1F hub. Dragon Stairs face east. |
| **Dragon Stairs** | Long (north), Bueller/Connector (north), Drug Lab/Drugs (north), Yellow Stairs (north), Dragon (south), Maintenance (south) | Dragon Stairs descent area. Primary vertical access. Now faces east per rework. |
| **Dragon Stairs Area** | Dragon Stairs (west), Gong (west), Maintenance (east) | Dragon stairs landing area. Circulation zone. |
| **Maintenance** | Dragon (north), Dragon Stairs (north), Dragon Stairs Area (north), Throne (north), Bumper (east) | Maintenance/mechanical area. Equipment storage. |

### Gong / Arcade wing (east/north)
| Room | Adjacent Rooms | Notes |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **Gong** | Drugs Hall (west), Bueller/Connector (west), Armory (west), Gong Dragon Banion (south), Dragon Stairs Area (south) | Gong room. New main entrance (Y4S4 rework). Carnival area. |
| **Gong Dragon Banion** | Gong (north), Throne (north), Bumper (south) | Gong-Dragon connection area. Banion/support structure. |
| **Arcade** | Toile (south), Arcade Stairs/Red (west), (connections) | Arcade game room area. Carnival play space. |
| **Arcade Stairs/Red** | Drug Lab/Drugs (north), Yellow Stairs (north), Arcade (east), Toile (south) | Arcade-to-basement staircase. Red callout zone. Vertical access. |
| **Toile** | Arcade (north), Arcade Stairs/Red (north) | Toile/fabric area. Carnival equipment storage. |
| **Yellow Stairs** | Drug Boiler (north), Yellow Boiler (north), Drug Lab/Drugs (north), Arcade Stairs/Red (east) | Yellow Stairs descent. Secondary vertical access. |
| **Bumper** | Gong Dragon Banion (north), Maintenance (west), Lockers (south) | Bumper cars carnival ride area. |
| **Lockers** | Bumper (north), Toile (south) | Locker/equipment room. |

---

## Key Adjacencies by Bomb Site

### Site 1 — Office / Initiation (2F)
**Attacker approach rooms:** Top Yellow, Cafe Hall, Waiting Room area
**Defender roam territory:** Top Yellow, Vault, Heaven area
**Vertical access:** Top Yellow connects to 1F below

### Site 2 — Bunk / Day Care (2F)
**Attacker approach rooms:** Cafe Hall, April, Yellow Hall
**Defender roam territory:** Cafe Hall, Break Room, April area
**Vertical access:** Yellow Hall area above 1F

### Site 3 — Armory / Throne (1F)
**Attacker approach rooms:** Long, Bueller/Connector, Dragon area
**Defender roam territory:** Red Hall, Gong, Dragon area
**Vertical access:** Dragon Stairs (primary); Arcade Stairs secondary

### Site 4 — Lab / Storage (1F)
**Primary attacker approach:** From upper floors via vertical descent
**Secondary approach:** Through Drugs Hall, Red Hall entry
**Defender roam territory:** Drugs Hall, Red Hall, Long corridor
**Vertical access:** Dragon Stairs, Yellow Stairs, Arcade Stairs

---

## Staircase Reference

| Name | Location | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|
| **Dragon Stairs** | Central 1F → 2F connection | Primary staircase. Central vertical axis. Now faces east (Y4S4). |
| **Arcade Stairs / Red** | 1F Arcade area → Basement-level descent | Secondary staircase. West-side vertical access. |
| **Yellow Stairs** | 1F Yellow area → Basement lower | Tertiary staircase. West-side vertical descent. |

---

## Key Vertical Relationships

| 2F Room | Roughly Above (1F) |
|---------|-------------------|
| Office / Initiation | Gong / Armory area |
| Top Yellow | Long / Bueller area |
| Vault | Long corridor |
| Cafe | Drug Lab area |
| Bunk / Day Care | Arcade / Bumper area |
| April | Bumper cars area |
| Yellow Hall | Bumper / Maintenance area |

---

## Y4S4 Operation Shifting Tides Rework Changes

**Major Layout Shifts:**
- **Rail platform removal:** Entire train, rail overlook, lift car, and ramps removed to reduce spawnpeek lines of sight and open sightlines.
- **East/West building closer:** East and West sections brought closer with new breakable wall connections (Yellow Corridor / Bunk connected to Office / Initiation).
- **Downstairs remodel:** Downstairs area completely remodeled with new bomb sites.

**Bomb Site Changes:**
- **Old Sites Replaced:** Gargoyle site → Armory (new), Haunting Dining → Throne (new)
- **New 1F sites:** Armory / Throne (1F) replaces previous ground floor objectives
- **2F sites maintained:** Office/Initiation, Bunk/Day Care remain on 2F (with layout refinements)

**New Rooms/Areas:**
- **Gong Room:** New main entrance (Y4S4). Replaces Arcade Entrance (which is now blocked).
- **Dragon area expansion:** Dragon Stairs now faces east (directional change from original).

**Connectivity Changes:**
- Yellow Corridor / Bunk area connected to Office / Initiation via new breakable walls
- Reduced spawnpeek vulnerability through layout repositioning
- Tighter east-west connectivity

---

strats.siteContext: Most played site on Villa (655 rounds on siege.gg). 60% defense WR — defender-sided. Community default comfort pick. Top priority to have a developed strat.
strats.content:
# Villa — Planes/Aviator / Games/Pool (2F) · ATTACK

**Status:** ✅ Fully Developed
**Formation:** 3-1-1
**Developed:** 2026-03-08
**Data Source:** siege.gg (Y11S1) + team input

⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21 — STRAT REQUIRES OPERATOR REASSIGNMENT:** This strat was built around Slug's Hibana (X-KAIROS) for the 90 window / Vault wall breach. **Peej does not run Hibana (Ash/Brava main).** Before executing this strat with the current roster, assign the Vault wall breach to Sarge (Hibana) and put Peej in the entry fragger role pushing through the Classical Hall wall instead. Alternatively, have Smigs run Ace (S.E.L.M.A.) to open both walls and shift Peej to the 90-window entry pressure role. The strat is still viable — the dual-breach mechanic is what makes it powerful — but Peej's role and operator must be reassigned before use. Villa is currently ❌ Avoid in Y11S1 — contingency use only.

> **Site Context:** Most played site on Villa (655 rounds on siege.gg). 60% defense WR — defender-sided. Community default comfort pick. Top priority to have a developed strat.

---

## Spawn

3-man group spawns at the spawn closest to site (front/courtyard side). Two solos each take one of the remaining two spawns.

---

## Roles

| Player | Role | Operator | Group |
|--------|------|----------|-------|
| **Sarge** | Entry Fragger / Study Lead | Zofia (primary) / Flex | 3-man |
| **Smigs** | Hard Breach | Thermite or Ace | 3-man |
| **Hound** | Anti-gadget | Thatcher | 3-man |
| **Peej** | Entry Fragger / 90 Window | Ash (⚠️ Hibana reassignment needed — see header) | Solo |
| **Grant** | IGL / Backside Flex | Flex | Solo |

**Sarge** — Leads Study clear and is first through the Classical Hall→Games/Pool wall breach.

**Smigs** — Places and detonates the Exothermic Charge / SELMA on the Classical Hall→Games/Pool wall (the "main wall"). Coordinates detonation timing with Peej's "wall ready" call.

**Hound** — Throws EMPs on the main wall before Smigs plants charge. If intel suggests Bandit/Kaid on Vault wall, throw a spare EMP toward the 90 window side to assist Peej.

**Peej** — Solo on 90 window. Fires X-KAIROS pellets on the single Vault wall into Games/Pool. Calls "wall ready" when pellets are set.

**Grant** — Solo backside push through basement approach. Drones to confirm backside roamer before executing. Catches defenders rotating away from site during the breach.

---

## Execute Sequence

1. **Drone phase** — Grant advances through basement approach and drones the backside to confirm roamer position. 3-man drones Study/upper approach. Peej drones toward 90 window.
2. **Study clear** — 3-man (Sarge/Smigs/Hound) clears Study and Classical Hall to deny roamer flank on the main wall approach.
3. **EMP** — Hound throws EMP(s) on the Classical Hall→Games/Pool wall to destroy Bandit/Kaid gadgets. Throw spare toward Vault wall if intel suggests electrical device there.
4. **Charge set** — Smigs places charge on the Classical Hall→Games/Pool wall and calls "charge set." Peej moves to 90 window and places Hibana pellets on the Vault wall; calls "wall ready."
5. **Simultaneous breach** — Smigs detonates charge; Peej fires pellets. Both walls open at the same time.
6. **Entry** — Sarge pushes first through the Classical Hall→Games/Pool wall into Games/Pool. Hound and Smigs follow immediately.
7. **Backside** — Grant pushes up from basement backside to catch any defenders repositioning away from site during the breach.
8. **Plant** — Plant in the Games/Pool, ideally behind the bar counter for cover on multiple angles.

---

## Plant Position (PP)

**Primary:** Games/Pool — behind bar counter (cover from Vault angle and Aviator side)
**Alternate:** Planes/Aviator side — if anchors are already cleared from that half of site

---

## Post-Plant

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Sarge** | Games/Pool | Holds cross angles through site |
| **Hound** | Vault wall breach | Covers the opening to prevent re-entry |
| **Smigs** | Red stairs / hallway | Watches for roamer pushing in post-plant |
| **Peej** | Repositions off 90 window | Joins site perimeter or covers exterior once pellets are fired |
| **Grant** | Basement backside | Holds rotation path; denies flanking the plant |

---

## Watch For

- **Red stairs roamer** — one of the most common defender positions. Smigs covers from hallway side, Grant covers from backside. Identify and trade early if possible.

- **Bandit / Kaid on the Classical Hall→Games/Pool wall** — Hound EMPs first. If the charge is fizzled, wait for Hound's second EMP or fall back to a soft floor alternative. Do not waste the charge.

- **Bandit / Kaid on the Vault wall** — Peej's Hibana pellets can be blocked if Bandit successfully tricks the wall. Hound should throw a spare EMP toward the 90 window side if intel suggests it. Peej confirms pellet placement before committing.

- **Mira Black Mirror on the main wall** — Smigs' Thermite/Ace charge destroys both the wall and the mirror simultaneously. This is expected — do not hesitate on the breach. Call it so the team knows Mira's intel coverage is gone.

- **Mira Black Mirror on the Vault wall** — Peej's Hibana pellets also destroy both wall and mirror. Call it immediately ("Mira gone, Vault wall open") so the team updates their defensive read.

- **Azami (Kiba Barriers)** — S-tier community pick. Can place Kiba Barriers on the Vault wall side window, Aviator windows, or site interior to reshape geometry mid-round. A Kiba Barrier placed on the 90-window approach forces Peej to destroy it or reposition before firing Hibana pellets. More flexible than Mira and increasingly common in Y11S1.

- **Fenrir (F-NATT gas)** — A-tier, frequently banned. If present, expect F-NATT canisters inside Games/Pool near the plant spot. Pop them before planting — Sarge or Hound should have grenades to detonate canisters before approaching the defuser.

- **Lesion (Gu mines)** — A-tier. Gu mines seeded throughout Gallery and Study approach — less immediately lethal than Kapkan but constant tempo disruption. The ping when hit tells Lesion the team's position. Move through approach paths with intent; don't sprint.

- **Mute (Signal Disruptors)** — A-tier. Mute jammers near the Vault wall or 90-window position can prevent Peej's Hibana pellets from arming. If Peej calls "pellets not arming," suspect Mute — Hound should drone to locate the jammer before Peej repositions.

- **Echo (Yokai drone)** — D-tier in Y11S1 meta and rarely played at high rank. Historically appeared on this site; if seen, expect Yokai disorientation pulses during the breach entry. Increasingly uncommon — do not plan around this.

- **3-anchor defense** — typical setup is one anchor in Vault (hard angle), one behind bar (cross into Games/Pool), one flexible in Games/Pool / Planes/Aviator. Drone pre-execute to identify positions before committing to the push.

---

## Win Conditions

There is more than one way to win this site. The execute sequence above is **Win A**. If conditions change mid-round, call the audible early — don't burn time trying to force a denied path.

---

### Win A — Primary: Simultaneous Double Breach ✅
**When:** Hibana alive, Vault wall accessible, Thermite/Ace alive, Hound EMP connects on Classical Hall→Games/Pool wall.

This is the default. Both walls open at the same time, Sarge enters first, Grant creates chaos from backside. Plant in Games/Pool.

**Who calls it:** Grant calls the go from backside once he has confirmed the roamer position. If Grant has no comms, Peej calls "wall ready" and Smigs detonates on that call.

---

### Win B — Vault Wall Denied ⚠️
**When:** Hibana is banned, Peej is dead, Bandit tricks the Vault wall and Hound has no EMPs left, or the Vault wall position is physically inaccessible.

**Adjustment:**
- Drop the Vault wall entirely. Do not waste time or gadgets on it.
- 3-man opens the main wall only. Sarge pushes through. Hound and Smigs follow.
- Grant pushes harder from backside — his job shifts from "chaos" to "necessary pressure" since we're entering from one direction only.
- Plant goes in **Planes/Aviator** instead of Games/Pool (reduces cross exposure from the Vault side).
- Post-plant: Peej repositions to cover the Vault wall intact angle from the exterior. The undestroyed Vault wall becomes a known defender position — don't let anyone sit in it unchallenged.

**Who calls it:** Peej calls "Vault denied, going single breach" the moment the wall is confirmed inaccessible. Sarge confirms the adjusted plant position.

---

### Win C — Main Wall Denied ⚠️
**When:** Thermite and Ace are both banned, Smigs is dead, or Bandit/Kaid fully denies the main wall with no EMP coverage remaining.

**Adjustment:**
- Do not charge a denied wall. Call the abort immediately — wasting a Thermite charge into a Bandit trick is a lost round.
- Shift to a **soft wall / floor alternative.** Study has soft walls — a Sledge, Buck, or any hard breach on a soft wall adjacent to the site can create a new angle.
- Peej's Hibana pellets on the Vault wall may now become the **primary** entry point if the main wall is shut. Peej fires pellets, creates the opening, and the whole team stacks toward the Vault entry instead.
- If both walls are denied and no soft alternative exists: play for picks, not the breach. Grant's backside and a numbers advantage reset the round — force a gunfight rather than a failed execute.

**Who calls it:** Smigs calls "wall denied, abort execute" immediately after a failed breach attempt. Grant then calls the alternate approach.

---

### Win D — Early Man Advantage (Backside Chaos)
**When:** Grant gets one or more kills during his backside push before the execute, creating a 5v4 or 5v3 before the breach.

**Adjustment:**
- This is a **gift round** — don't slow down to plan. Push faster.
- If Grant gets the backside roamer early, call it immediately: "Roamer dead, execute now." The team should not wait for full drone readiness if there's already a numbers advantage.
- The main breach goes exactly as Win A, but with less threat on site post-plant. Sarge pushes more aggressively knowing site is thin.

**Who calls it:** Grant calls every kill and announces man-advantage immediately. It's on Grant to accelerate the tempo when this happens.

---

## Audibles

- **Study locked / can't push through:** Fall back to a perimeter squeeze. Hold spawn control and wait for Grant's backside kill to shift defenders' attention before re-engaging Study.
- **Both Peej and Smigs dead early:** Go to a pick-based round. Don't attempt the execute with no breach utility — play for a 3v5 opportunistic win from crossfire angles.
- **Fenrir canisters blocking plant spot:** Call it. Sarge or Hound needs a grenade or explosive to pop the canister before approaching the defuser. Do not plant into an active F-NATT canister — it will cost the round.
