The team's historical problem on this site: 1–2 early deaths to unexpected roamers before the execute even begins.
strats.content:
# Villa — Kitchen / Dining Room (1F) · ATTACK

**Status:** ✅ Fully Developed
**Formation:** 2-2-1
**Developed:** 2026-03-09 (rewritten — map verified)
**Data Source:** siege.gg (Y11S1) + team input + Villa_1F.png + Villa_2F.png
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej replaces Slug. No Hibana dependency on this strat — name swap only. Peej's Ash/Brava entry fragger identity adds aggressive entry pressure to the Laundry approach. Villa is currently ❌ Avoid in Y11S1 — contingency use only.

> **Site Context:** 60% defense WR (355 rounds on siege.gg) — defender-sided. This site almost exclusively appears as the third site option (after two lost attacks), meaning defenders know it's coming and will be set up and ready. The team's historical problem on this site: 1–2 early deaths to unexpected roamers before the execute even begins.
>
> **Map layout note:** Kitchen (4A) and Dining Room (4B) are connected internally through China — a small transition room between them. The primary attacker breach point is the **Laundry → Dining Room wall** on the east side of the building. Vertical access from 2F (Astronomy above Kitchen, Statue/Connector area above Dining) is the force-multiplier that makes the execute work.

---

## Spawn

Not yet locked in. Recommend the spawn that gives the cleanest approach to Laundry (east side of building) without early exposure. Back Stairs exterior access is also viable for the vertical pair. Confirm and log after first reps.

---

## Roles

| Player | Role | Operator | Group |
|--------|------|----------|-------|
| **Sarge** | Entry Fragger / Laundry Lead | Zofia (primary) / Flex | Laundry crew (2-man) |
| **Smigs** | Hard Breach | Thermite or Ace | Laundry crew (2-man) |
| **Peej** | Vertical / Floor Control | Buck or Sledge | Vertical pair (2-man) |
| **Hound** | Vertical / Floor Control | Buck or Sledge | Vertical pair (2-man) |
| **Grant** | IGL / Roam Clear | Flex | Solo |

**Sarge** — Leads the approach through Laundry. First through the Laundry→Dining Room breach. Secures and holds Dining Room once the wall is open. The Laundry crew's job ends at Dining — vertical pair owns Kitchen from above.

**Smigs** — Hard breach on the Laundry→Dining Room wall. Does NOT place the charge until Sarge has confirmed Laundry is clear of immediate threat and the Aruni gate (if present) is destroyed. Uses frag grenade to destroy any Aruni gate on the Laundry doorframe before placing the charge.

**Peej + Hound (Vertical pair)** — Get to 2F via Back Stairs or Main Stairs as fast as possible. Position in Astronomy (above Kitchen) and/or Connector/Sofa area (above Dining). Open floors from above and shoot down into site. Two players on vertical is intentional — one opens the floor, one shoots. Call "overhead ready" when they have angles into site.

**Grant** — IGL. Approaches from the Back Hall or Skylight Hall side. Primary job: find and contain the roamer before anyone else commits. Calls roamer position throughout. Closes rotation post-execute.

---

## Execute Sequence

1. **Drone phase — mandatory.** All five players drone before moving. Priority: find the roamer (Vigil in Skylight Hall / Dining area — this is Sarge's own DEF pick on this site; he knows the routes). Peej and Hound also drone toward 2F access to confirm no defender is contesting vertical.

2. **Peej and Hound break for 2F** — immediately after drone phase, get to 2F via Back Stairs (fastest to Astronomy) or Main Stairs. Destination: Astronomy (above Kitchen) and Connector/Statue side (above Dining Room). Do not take a bad fight getting upstairs — position fast but clean.

3. **Grant positions on Back Hall or Skylight Hall** — cuts off the roamer's escape path. Calls roamer position as drones find it. Does not push into Dining — watches the flank.

4. **Sarge and Smigs approach Laundry** — patient movement from the east exterior side. Watch for Kapkan traps in Laundry and Memorial area. Drone the Laundry→Dining Room doorframe/wall to check for Aruni gate.

5. **Aruni gate check** — if Smigs identifies an Aruni gate on the Laundry→Dining Room doorframe, Smigs throws a frag grenade to destroy it before placing the Thermite charge. Do not place charge until the gate is gone.

6. **Vertical pair calls "overhead ready"** — Peej or Hound calls when they have a floor breach and are shooting into Kitchen or Dining Room from above. This is the execute signal.

7. **Smigs places charge on the Laundry→Dining Room wall; calls "charge set."** Execute is simultaneous with vertical ready.

8. **Smigs detonates** on "overhead ready" call. Sarge enters Dining Room first through the Laundry breach. Vertical pair shoots down through floors to force defenders off their positions.

9. **Sarge clears Dining Room** — holds the breach and secures Dining Room. Smigs follows to cover the Laundry opening. The Laundry crew holds Dining — they do not push further. Vertical pair handles Kitchen from above.

10. **Grant closes rotation** — pushes from Back Hall or Skylight Hall to cut off any defender repositioning away from site.

11. **Plant** in Kitchen (4A) or Dining Room (4B) depending on remaining defender positions.

---

## Plant Position (PP)

**Primary:** Kitchen (4A) — furthest from the Laundry breach entry; use cover inside Kitchen (appliances/counters)
**Alternate:** Dining Room (4B) — if Kitchen is still contested; plant near China doorway cover

---

## Post-Plant

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Sarge** | Kitchen interior | Holds cross angles through site |
| **Smigs** | Dining Room | Covers Laundry breach opening; denies re-entry |
| **Peej** | Hatch above Kitchen (Astronomy side) | Stays on vertical; denies defuse from above |
| **Hound** | Hatch above Dining Room (Statue/Connector side) | Overhead denial on defuse attempts |
| **Grant** | Back Hall / Skylight Hall | Holds rotation path denial post-plant |

> The vertical pair staying on hatches post-plant is the key win condition. Anyone trying to defuse has overhead exposure from two positions above — Kitchen and Dining both covered.

---

## Win Conditions

There is more than one way to win this site. The execute sequence above is **Win A**. Call audibles early — don't burn time forcing a denied path.

---

### Win A — Primary: Vertical + Laundry Breach ✅
**When:** Peej and Hound establish floor control above site, Smigs has a clean Laundry→Dining wall to breach, roamer is located or contained by Grant.

Vertical pressure forces defenders to look up and shift off site positions. Sarge's Laundry breach hits a split defense. Both threats hit simultaneously — the anchor(s) can't cover both the floor above and the Laundry side at the same time.

**Who calls it:** Peej or Hound calls "overhead ready." Smigs detonates on that call.

---

### Win B — Vertical Blocked (Defenders Contest 2F Above Site) ⚠️
**When:** Defenders send a roamer to the 2F Astronomy / Statue/Connector area to deny Peej and Hound.

**Adjustment:**
- Grant's Back Hall / Back Stairs position becomes critical — a defender going upstairs past Astronomy has to pass near the Back Stairs exterior. Grant or Peej should be able to intercept.
- If Peej and Hound are being contested, they clear the upstairs roamer before opening floors — do not rush the hatch with a defender lurking above.
- If vertical is completely denied: fall to a ground-only push. Sarge leads the Laundry breach, Smigs follows, Hound repositions downstairs to push through Back Hall side. Three-man ground push from two directions.

**Who calls it:** Peej/Hound call if they're being contested upstairs. Grant calls any Back Stairs movement spotted.

---

### Win C — Laundry Wall Denied (Bandit / Kaid) ⚠️
**When:** Bandit or Kaid electrifies the Laundry→Dining Room wall and Smigs can't breach cleanly.

**Adjustment:**
- Vertical becomes the primary entry. Peej and Hound use Buck/Sledge to open a large enough floor breach above Dining Room or Kitchen to shoot AND drop into site.
- Sarge repositions from Laundry to the Memorial / Skylight Hall approach — enters Dining Room from below/south side instead.
- Smigs shifts to support Sarge on the Memorial approach — looks for a soft wall entry from that side.

**Who calls it:** Smigs calls "wall denied" the moment the charge fails. Grant calls the alternate approach.

---

### Win D — Early Roamer Kill ⚠️
**When:** Grant kills the roamer early from Back Hall or Skylight Hall, creating a 5v4 before the execute.

**Adjustment:** Call it and compress. "Roamer dead, execute now." Don't wait for the full vertical setup — Sarge can push the Laundry breach faster with a numbers advantage.

**Who calls it:** Grant calls every kill immediately. No delay.

---

## Watch For

- **Kapkan traps in Laundry and Dining Room** — standard trap zone on this site. Smigs and Sarge move through Laundry slowly. Call traps as found. Do not sprint through the doorframe into Dining even after the wall is open — Kapkan traps inside Dining Room arches are the primary pre-execute kill mechanism defenders rely on.

- **Vigil roamer (Sarge's own DEF pick here)** — silent, no audio cue. Sarge knows this player's exact tendencies — Vigil will be in Skylight Hall or Dining Room early, then disengage toward Back Hall or Laundry. Grant's drone phase on Skylight Hall / Back Hall is specifically to find this player before anyone commits.

- **Aruni gate on the Laundry→Dining Room doorframe** — Smigs must check for this before placing the charge. Frag grenade destroys it. Without Thatcher on this formation, there's no EMP — physical destruction (grenade / gunfire) is the only option. Do not place the Thermite charge while an active Aruni gate is in the frame.

- **Bandit / Kaid on the Laundry→Dining Room wall** — Win C scenario. Without Thatcher, a successful trick on this wall means no Laundry breach. Call it immediately and shift to vertical entry.

- **China as a close-quarters kill zone** — China connects Dining Room to Kitchen. Defenders will frequently hold this tight corridor to cut off any push toward Kitchen. This is not Sarge and Smigs' problem — they hold Dining. But Grant or the vertical pair pushing to finish off Kitchen need to be aware of the China angle. Drone it before anyone pushes through.

- **Defenders contesting 2F vertical** — watch Back Stairs movement during drone phase. If a defender goes to Astronomy or the Connector area, Peej/Hound need to deal with them before the floor breach. Call it as soon as it's spotted.

- **Mute (Signal Disruptors)** — A-tier. A Mute jammer near the Laundry→Dining Room wall prevents Smigs' Thermite charge from detonating. If the charge fizzles, suspect Mute. Smigs must locate and destroy the jammer (drone it, shoot through the wall if possible) before attempting a second charge.

- **Fenrir (F-NATT gas)** — A-tier, frequently banned. If present, expect F-NATT canisters inside Kitchen near the plant spot and China access. Pop them before Sarge pushes through — an active canister during the push is a free defender kill.

- **Third-site defender aggression** — when defenders pick this as the last site, expect tighter holds and possibly double roamers. Respect the setup. The drone phase is not optional.

---

## Audibles

- **Both Peej and Hound killed before vertical is established:** No vertical threat. Collapse to a 3-man ground push — Sarge leads Laundry breach, Grant closes the flank from Skylight Hall, Smigs supports. Still a viable push, just slower.
- **Sarge killed in Laundry approach:** Smigs does not solo push through the breach. Fall back. Regroup with Grant and look for an opportunity pick from the Back Hall side before re-engaging.
- **Both Smigs and vertical blocked simultaneously:** No execute. Play for picks from exterior angles and reset. This site at 60% DEF WR without any utility is a gunfight the defenders are winning.

strats.siteContext: 43% defense WR (58 rounds on siege.gg) — the most attacker-friendly site on Villa. Defenders shun this site because the vertical hatch access from above creates pressure they can't reliably stop. If an opponent picks Living/Library, it's a gift — they're voluntarily playing the least defensible site on the map. Approach with confidence but don't get sloppy.
strats.content:
# Villa — Living Room / Library (1F) · ATTACK

**Status:** ✅ Developed (contingency strat — use if defenders pick this site)
**Formation:** 2-2-1
**Developed:** 2026-03-08
**Data Source:** siege.gg (Y11S1) + site layout analysis
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej replaces Slug. No Hibana dependency on this strat — name swap only. Villa is currently ❌ Avoid in Y11S1 — contingency use only.

> **Site Context:** 43% defense WR (58 rounds on siege.gg) — the most attacker-friendly site on Villa. Defenders shun this site because the vertical hatch access from above creates pressure they can't reliably stop. If an opponent picks Living/Library, it's a gift — they're voluntarily playing the least defensible site on the map. Approach with confidence but don't get sloppy.
>
> **No team data on this site** — neither attacking nor defending. Build reps before relying on this strat in a clutch game.

---

## Why Vertical Wins This Site

Living/Library has multiple hatch access points from the floor above. This is the primary reason defenders avoid picking it — attackers who own the floor above can shoot down into site freely while the ground team pushes in from the stairs. The defense has no clean answer to simultaneous overhead pressure and a ground push. The strat is built entirely around exploiting this.

---

## Roles

| Player | Role | Operator | Group |
|--------|------|----------|-------|
| **Sarge** | Ground Entry / Stairs Lead | Zofia (primary) / Flex | Ground (2-man) |
| **Smigs** | Ground Breach Support | Thermite or Ace | Ground (2-man) |
| **Peej** | Vertical / Hatch Control | Buck or Sledge | Vertical (2-man) |
| **Hound** | Vertical / Hatch Control | Buck or Sledge | Vertical (2-man) |
| **Grant** | IGL / Flank Watch | Flex | Solo |

**Sarge** — Leads the ground-level stairs approach into Living Room. Moves slowly — main stairs likely have Kapkan traps at top and bottom. First through the ground entry once vertical pressure is established.

**Smigs** — Supports Sarge on the ground approach. Brings Thermite/Ace in case the connecting wall between Living Room and Library is reinforced. Also watches the ground flank for any roamer pushing the stair approach.

**Peej + Hound (Vertical pair)** — Get to the floor above Living/Library early. Use Buck or Sledge to open the hatches from above. Once hatches are open, shoot down into site to create overhead pressure and force defenders to move or die. This is the round-winning play on this site. Two players on vertical is intentional — one opens, one shoots.

**Grant** — IGL. Holds the exterior or hallway flank approach. Watches for any roamer trying to exit the site and flanks the ground team. Calls intel throughout — defenders on this site may roam aggressively since they know the site is hard to hold.

---

## Execute Sequence

1. **Drone phase** — All players drone before moving. This site has no established data — drone carefully. Identify roamer position and anchor count before committing anyone.

2. **Peej and Hound break for vertical immediately** — get to the floor above Living/Library as fast as possible without taking a bad fight. The vertical pair's job is to own the hatches before the ground team pushes.

3. **Grant positions on the exterior or hallway flank** — watches for roamers exiting the site and covers the ground team's flank.

4. **Sarge and Smigs approach the main stairs slowly** — watch for Kapkan traps at the stair landing. Call traps as found. Do NOT push until vertical is ready.

5. **Vertical pair calls "overhead ready"** — Peej or Hound calls when they have a hatch open and are shooting into site. This is the signal.

6. **Ground push on the "overhead ready" call** — Sarge leads up the stairs into Living Room. Smigs follows. Defenders are split between the overhead threat and the stair push.

7. **Smigs places Thermite charge** on the Living↔Library connecting wall if reinforced — breaches the second room while Sarge holds Living Room.

8. **Grant pushes to close off rotation** once the ground team is in site.

9. **Plant** — plant in whichever room is clearer based on drone intel and the vertical pair's callouts.

---

## Plant Position (PP)

**Primary:** Living Room — use furniture or wall cover away from the hatch openings (vertical pair stays on hatches to deny defuse)
**Alternate:** Library — if Living Room anchor is still alive and holding

---

## Post-Plant

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Sarge** | Living Room | Holds cross angles; covers defuse |
| **Smigs** | Living↔Library breach or stairs | Watches stair re-entry and defuse angles |
| **Peej** | Hatch above | Stays on hatch — shoots anyone attempting defuse from above |
| **Hound** | Second hatch above | Denies second approach from overhead |
| **Grant** | Exterior / Hallway | Holds rotation denial post-plant |

> The vertical pair staying on hatches post-plant is the key win condition. Anyone trying to defuse has overhead exposure from two hatch positions — almost impossible to defuse safely.

---

## Win Conditions

### Win A — Primary: Vertical Pressure + Ground Push ✅
**When:** Peej and Hound establish hatch control, shoot into site, and the ground team pushes while defenders are pinned.

This is the default and should work most of the time on a 43% DEF WR site. The overhead pressure alone forces defenders out of position. Sarge's ground push cleans up.

**Who calls it:** Peej or Hound calls "overhead ready" when hatch is open and they have angles into site. Sarge advances on that call.

---

### Win B — Vertical Blocked (Defenders Contest the Floor Above) ⚠️
**When:** Defenders send a roamer upstairs to deny Peej and Hound from getting hatch control.

**Adjustment:** Grant's flank watch becomes critical — the roamer going upstairs is the player Grant should be able to intercept from the exterior/hallway. If Grant catches the upstairs roamer, call it and vertical proceeds. If not, Peej and Hound need to clear the roamer on the upper floor before opening hatches — do not rush the hatch with a defender lurking above.

**Who calls it:** Grant calls any upstairs roamer spotted. Peej/Hound call if they're being contested.

---

### Win C — Connecting Wall Denied (Bandit/Kaid) ⚠️
**When:** The Living↔Library connecting wall is reinforced and electrified, and Hound has no EMPs.

**Adjustment:** Don't breach — play the site from one room only. Vertical pressure on Living Room is enough to win a plant there. The Library becomes a closed room defenders may anchor — vertical pair identifies the Library angle and blocks it with hatch coverage. Smigs shifts to support Sarge in Living rather than breaching Library.

---

### Win D — Early Roamer Kill ⚠️
**When:** Grant kills the roamer before the execute, or Peej/Hound catch the upstairs roamer early.

**Adjustment:** Call it and compress the execute. With a numbers advantage, the ground push and vertical push can happen simultaneously without waiting for full hatch setup.

---

## Watch For

> ⚠️ **No team data on this site and very limited community data (58r on siege.gg).** These notes are based on site layout and general Villa defender tendencies. Update after the team gets reps here.

- **Roamer going upstairs to contest vertical** — the one reliable defender counter to this strat. Grant and the vertical pair must communicate. If someone goes upstairs, find them before opening hatches.

- **Azami (Kiba Barriers) on hatch edges** — Azami can place Kiba Barriers directly over or around hatch openings, sealing them from above or reshaping the vertical angles. Peej and Hound should check for Kiba Barriers before attempting to open hatches — destroying them requires grenades or sustained fire. A sealed hatch turns Win A into an improvised ground-only push.

- **Kapkan traps on the main stairs** — Sarge moves slowly up the stairs. These traps are a community-wide standard on stair entry sites. Call them and step around rather than sprinting through.

- **Community DEF picks to expect (Y11S1 meta):** On any site with no team data, assume the following based on current tier list — Smoke (S-tier, post-plant denial — nearly universal), Jäger (S-tier, throwable denial — 80%+ pick rate), Valkyrie (S-tier, cameras), Azami (S-tier, Kiba Barriers on windows and hatch edges to disrupt vertical setup), Bandit/Kaid (A-tier, wall electrification), Lesion (A-tier, Gu mines on stair approach), Fenrir (A-tier, high ban rate; if not banned, F-NATT gas in site). Echo is D-tier in Y11S1 and rarely played at high rank — remove it from planning assumptions. Update this list after the team builds reps here.

- **Aggressive defender anchor** — on a rarely-played site, some defenders will play unusually aggressive angles that aren't standard because there's no established "correct" way to defend it. Drone carefully and don't assume conventional anchor positions.

- **Reinforced connecting wall** — if defenders reinforce the Living↔Library wall, the round becomes a one-room execute. Smigs brings Thermite as a fallback; Hound can EMP if gadgets are on the wall.

---

## Audibles

- **Vertical pair can't get upstairs safely:** Abort vertical. Collapse to a 4-man ground push — all players approach from main stairs. This is less efficient but Living/Library is attacker-favored enough that 4v5 ground pushes can still win.
- **Both vertical players die before hatches open:** The round becomes a standard ground push. Sarge leads, Smigs supports, Grant closes the flank. No overhead threat — play clean gunfights and use the attacker-favorable site layout.
- **Defender picks surprise you (non-standard setup):** Slow down, drone more. Do not rush an execute based on assumptions when defender positions are unknown on a site with no data.

---

## Future Notes

_Update this strat after the team gets reps on Living/Library — either attacking or defending. Key things to log:_
- _Spawn that gives cleanest vertical access_
- _Which hatches above the site are most useful_
- _Common defender picks observed on this site_
- _Whether the connecting wall is typically reinforced_

strats.siteContext: 51% defense WR (488 rounds on siege.gg) — the most balanced site on Villa. Neither side has a structural edge; execution quality decides the round. Second-most-played site on the map. Bedroom wall is almost always fully reinforced; head holes are possible but rare.
strats.content:
# Villa — Trophy / Statue (2F) · ATTACK

**Status:** ✅ Fully Developed
**Formation:** 3-1-1
**Developed:** 2026-03-08
**Data Source:** siege.gg (Y11S1) + team input
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej replaces Slug. Peej's entry fragger identity (Ash/Brava) pairs well with the solo pressure roles on this strat. Villa is currently ❌ Avoid in Y11S1 — contingency use only.

> **Site Context:** 51% defense WR (488 rounds on siege.gg) — the most balanced site on Villa. Neither side has a structural edge; execution quality decides the round. Second-most-played site on the map. Bedroom wall is almost always fully reinforced; head holes are possible but rare.

---

## Spawn

Adjust based on which spawn gives the cleanest access to the 2F bedroom approach. Not yet locked in — confirm which spawn the team defaults to as this strat gets reps.

---

## Roles

| Player | Role | Operator | Group |
|--------|------|----------|-------|
| **Sarge** | Entry Fragger | Zofia (primary) / Flex | 3-man |
| **Smigs** | Hard Breach | Thermite or Ace | 3-man |
| **Hound** | Anti-gadget | Thatcher | 3-man |
| **Peej / Grant** | Window / Crossfire | Flex frag op | Solo |
| **Grant / Peej** | IGL / Roamer Denial | Flex | Solo |

> **Note on Peej and Grant:** These two roles are interchangeable for the solo positions. Peej's Ash/Brava entry fragger identity maps well to the window/crossfire solo role — aggressive first-contact with good escape options. Grant plays the IGL/backside denial role. Lock in a default assignment and stick with it for consistency.

**Sarge** — Leads the bedroom clear and is first through the reinforced wall breach into Trophy.

**Smigs** — Places and detonates the Thermite Exothermic Charge / Ace SELMA on the reinforced bedroom wall. Coordinates detonation with the window player's "window set" call.

**Hound** — Throws EMPs on the bedroom wall before Smigs places the charge. Head holes are rare on this site but possible — Hound EMPs regardless as insurance, and it also counters any Bandit/Kaid gadgets on the wall.

**Solo (Peej or Grant) — Window position:** Takes the window near the stairs that looks toward Trophy/Statue. This is a **patience and timing role** — not a breach angle, purely a frag/crossfire position. The goal is to hold this spot, create pressure during the breach, and pick off any defender shifting position or peeking through site during the chaos. Calls "window set" when ready.

**Solo (Grant or Peej) — IGL / Roamer Denial:** Drones the hallway and stairwell areas to confirm roamer location. Pushes from an alternate angle or closes off the rotation path away from site as the breach goes off. Communicates roamer position throughout.

---

## Execute Sequence

1. **Drone phase** — Roamer-denial player drones hallway, stairs, and Study to confirm roamer. Window player drones toward site.
2. **Bedroom clear** — 3-man (Sarge/Smigs/Hound) clears the bedroom to deny any roamer from flanking Smigs while placing the charge.
3. **EMP** — Hound throws EMP(s) on the reinforced bedroom wall. Always — even when head holes aren't confirmed.
4. **Charge set** — Smigs places charge on the bedroom wall and calls "charge set." Window player moves to position and calls "window set."
5. **Execute** — Smigs detonates on the "window set" call. Window player applies simultaneous crossfire pressure toward Trophy/Statue.
6. **Entry** — Sarge pushes first through the breach into Trophy. Hound follows immediately.
7. **Roamer denial** — The roamer-denial solo closes off the rotation path as the breach happens — catches anyone trying to reposition away from site.
8. **Plant** — Plant in Trophy or Statue depending on anchor positions identified during drone phase.

---

## Plant Position (PP)

**Primary:** Trophy — near the "astronomy" desk cover (the anchor's typical hold position — they'll be dead or displaced by plant time)
**Alternate:** Statue side — if Trophy is still too hot after entry

---

## Post-Plant

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Sarge** | Trophy | Holds cross angles through site |
| **Hound** | Bedroom breach | Covers the wall opening; denies re-entry |
| **Smigs** | Hallway / stairwell side | Watches for roamer pushing in post-plant |
| **Peej / Grant** | Repositions from window | Joins site perimeter once breach is done |
| **Grant / Peej** | Alternate approach | Holds rotation path; denies flanking the plant |

---

## Win Conditions

### Win A — Primary: Bedroom Breach + Window Crossfire ✅
**When:** Bedroom wall accessible, Thatcher EMPs connect, Thermite/Ace alive, window player in position.

The simultaneous breach and window pressure split defender attention — the anchor can't hold both at once. Sarge pushes the disoriented defender, plant goes down.

**Who calls it:** Window player calls "window set" → Smigs detonates on that call.

---

### Win B — Wall Denied (Bandit / Kaid) ⚠️
**When:** Bandit/Kaid denies the bedroom wall and Hound has no EMPs remaining.

**Adjustment:** Call it immediately — do not waste the charge. Identify a soft wall alternative adjacent to Trophy/Statue and shift the push there. Window player maintains position — the crossfire angle is still valid regardless of which wall opens.

**Who calls it:** Smigs calls "wall denied, abort." Sarge or Grant calls the new wall.

---

### Win C — Window Player Gets Early Pick(s) ⚠️
**When:** The window solo gets a kill on a defender shifting through Trophy/Statue before the breach.

**Adjustment:** Call it and accelerate immediately. Do not wait for the full setup — push the man advantage now.

**Who calls it:** Window player calls every kill. Team pushes on that call.

---

### Win D — Roamer Killed Early ⚠️
**When:** The roamer-denial solo catches and kills the roamer during the drone/pre-execute phase.

**Adjustment:** "Roamer dead, push now." Accelerate the execute — 5v4 pre-breach is a major advantage, don't waste it sticking to the full timeline.

**Who calls it:** Grant/Peej calls every roamer kill immediately.

---

## Watch For

- **Anchor behind "astronomy" desk (Trophy)** — drone site before executing to confirm position. If the anchor has shifted, adjust the entry angle before Sarge pushes through.
- **Head holes on the bedroom wall (rare)** — Hound EMPs regardless; don't let Smigs plant into a wall with an active head hole angle.
- **Roamer in the stairwell or hallway** — most common roamer path on this side of the map. Roamer-denial player's primary job; confirm before 3-man commits to bedroom.
- **Bandit / Kaid on the bedroom wall** — Thatcher handles this. If Thatcher is banned, identify an alternate approach before attempting the breach.
- **Defender shifting from site during the breach** — window player watches for movement as the wall opens. A repositioning defender is the window player's kill.
- **Azami (Kiba Barriers)** — S-tier community pick. Kiba Barriers can be placed on the Trophy windows, bedroom breach angle, or the window position used by the solo. Destroys or repositions the crossfire angle. Hound should drone for Kiba Barriers in the window zone before the window player commits.
- **Fenrir (F-NATT gas)** — A-tier, frequently banned. If present, expect F-NATT canisters placed inside Trophy near the plant spot. Sarge should drone site interior to spot canisters — pop them with a grenade before planting.
- **Lesion (Gu mines)** — A-tier. Gu mines seeded in the bedroom approach and hallway. The chip damage and position ping slow the 3-man advance. Move deliberately through the bedroom; do not sprint.
- **Mute (Signal Disruptors)** — A-tier. Mute jammers placed near the bedroom wall can prevent Smigs' charge from detonating. If the charge fizzles, suspect Mute — Hound should throw a spare EMP toward the wall to clear it before a second attempt.

---

## Audibles

- **Bedroom can't be cleared (roamer camping it hard):** Pull back. Wait for the window player or roamer-denial player to get a pick that shifts the count, then re-engage.
- **Both Smigs and Hound dead before execute:** Don't attempt the breach. Play for picks from crossfire angles — the window position is still valid without the hard breach.

strats.siteContext: Most played site on Villa (655 rounds on siege.gg). 60% defense WR — team performs at extremely high rates here. This setup mirrors what's been working naturally — don't overcomplicate it.
strats.content:
# Villa — Planes/Aviator / Games/Pool (2F) · DEFENSE

**Status:** ✅ Fully Developed
**Developed:** 2026-03-06 (migrated from STACK_04 2026-03-08)
**Note:** Site names corrected from old KB ("Master Bedroom/Bathroom" was wrong). Correct names confirmed by map: Planes/Aviator / Games/Pool.
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej (Maestro/Valkyrie) replaces Slug (Smoke/Valkyrie). Post-plant denial transitions from Smoke canisters to Maestro Evil Eye + LMG. Peej must be positioned near his Evil Eye to operate it — less mobile than Smoke but provides simultaneous intel + denial. Caveira is Peej's primary DEF op; Maestro is secondary. Confirm Maestro placement angles on this site when next played. Villa is currently ❌ Avoid in Y11S1 — these strats are contingency use only.

> **Site Context:** Most played site on Villa (655 rounds on siege.gg). 60% defense WR — team performs at extremely high rates here. This setup mirrors what's been working naturally — don't overcomplicate it.

---

## Anchor Assignments

| Player | Operator | Position | Role |
|--------|----------|----------|------|
| **Hound** | Castle (or Frost / Azami) | Planes/Aviator | Primary anchor — barricades, holds Gallery/Hallway angle |
| **Peej** | Maestro or Valkyrie | Games/Pool | Post-plant denial (Smoke) or intel anchor (Valkyrie) |
| **Smigs** | Aruni | Aviator↔Games/Pool connecting area | Laser gate on connecting doorframe |
| **Grant** | Kapkan or Thorn (or Mute) | Gallery / Study approaches | Trap cluster on approach paths; mid-hall flex |
| **Sarge** | Vigil | Roamer (Balcony / 2F Hallway) | Silent roamer — pressure from rear, disengage to Study/Hall |

**Hound (Castle)** — Planes/Aviator. Barricades the Gallery-facing door and the Hallway approach. Holds from behind furniture using cover angles watching Gallery entrance and Hallway simultaneously. If Frost: beartraps behind the Aviator/Games/Pool connecting door and under the balcony window. If Azami: use Kiba Barriers on the Gallery-facing window and the Hallway approach instead of barricades — better against Sledge-heavy comps where Castle panels get destroyed quickly. Azami is emerging as Hound's strongest individual K/D DEF op in Y11S1 (7r / 71.4% / 2.33).

**Peej (Maestro / Valkyrie)** — If Maestro: anchors deep in Games/Pool; places Evil Eye cameras on the ceiling or wall above the plant spot — provides persistent intel + LMG denial without exposing himself. If Valkyrie: roams slightly into the Hall and comes back to anchor once cams are placed. ⚠️ Roster change 2026-03-21: Peej (Maestro/Valkyrie) replaces Slug (Smoke/Valkyrie). Post-plant denial mechanic changes — Evil Eye + LMG replaces Smoke canisters. Peej must stay near his Maestro Evil Eye to operate it.

**Smigs (Aruni)** — Aruni laser on the Aviator↔Games/Pool connecting doorframe — anyone breaching through takes the zap. Holds deep Games/Pool near the bar/corner.

**Grant (Kapkan / Thorn)** — Kapkan traps in the Gallery–Study access points (top of the stairs, Gallery entry). Thorn in the Planes/Aviator doorway area if they hard breach. Grant holds mid-hall to reinforce or redirect. If Mute: place Signal Disruptors at the Gallery drone entry points and near the main Aviator wall — prevents early attacker intel on anchor positions before the execute. Mute is Grant's actual #1 DEF op in Y11S1 (17r / 58.8%). Pick Mute when denying early drone coverage is more valuable than trap kills; pick Kapkan/Thorn when early kill pressure on the approach is the priority.

**Sarge (Vigil)** — Silent roamer. Opens from the Planes/Aviator balcony (rappel-side) or the 2F Hallway junction. Pressure from the rear. Disengage back into Study/Hall area when attackers are confirmed on site. **Does NOT hold inside site** — the four anchors own site; Sarge's only job is early picks and disengage.

---

## Gadget Deployment

- **Kapkan (Grant):** Gallery stairway entry, Study-facing door, Hall chokepoint — maximum trap density on approach paths
- **Aruni (Smigs):** Aviator↔Games/Pool connecting doorframe — forces attackers to destroy it or take damage; slows site traversal
- **Maestro (Peej):** Evil Eye camera placed on ceiling/wall above the Games/Pool plant spot — operate remotely for LMG denial of the defuse attempt. Place during setup phase; do not reveal it early
- **Frost alt (Hound):** If Frost over Castle, place beartraps behind the Planes/Aviator door and under the balcony window
- **Azami alt (Hound):** If Azami over Castle, Kiba Barriers on Gallery window and Hallway approach — use vs. Sledge-heavy comps
- **Mute alt (Grant):** If Mute over Kapkan/Thorn, Signal Disruptors at Gallery drone entry points and near the Aviator main wall — use when denying early attacker drone intel is the priority

---

## Reinforcement Priority

1. Planes/Aviator ↔ Gallery wall — seals off Gallery hard push (highest priority)
2. Study ↔ Hall wall — limits roamer flank exposure
3. Aviator ↔ Hallway secondary wall
4. Games/Pool exterior wall (if soft)

---

## Post-Plant Hold

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Peej (Maestro)** | Games/Pool depth | Denial from Evil Eye camera above plant spot; LMG fire on defuse attempt |
| **Hound** | Planes/Aviator depth | Watches Gallery breach; hard to shift |
| **Smigs** | Games/Pool far corner | Watches for defuser play from bar area |

---

## Win Conditions

### Win A — Primary: Trap-Gated Attrition Hold
**When:** Setup goes as planned — Kapkan traps hit in Gallery/Study, Aruni gate delays site entry, Sarge gets one early pick.

The traps change the round before the team even fires a shot. If Grant gets 1–2 trap kills in the approach phase, the team plays a 5v3 or 5v4 post-plant. Peej's Maestro Evil Eye wins the endgame.

### Win B — Sarge Roam Fails / Dies Early
**When:** Sarge takes a bad trade and dies in the first 30 seconds.

**Adjustment:** The four anchors tighten into site immediately. Do not chase with anyone else. Grant pulls back from his mid-hall position and anchors the Gallery side. The trap layer becomes even more important — let the traps do Sarge's damage. Peej's cameras become the primary intel source.

### Win C — Traps All Cleared / No Early Picks
**When:** Attackers drone out and manually clear every Kapkan trap before committing.

**Adjustment:** This is a pure gunfight round. Hound's Castle barricades become the last line of delay. Smigs' Aruni gate is the final attrition point. Peej should use Valkyrie for this site if trap denial is expected — intel advantage from extra cameras compensates for the lost Kapkan kills. Note: Maestro's Evil Eye still provides some LMG denial even without trap pressure.

---

## Watch For

- **Simultaneous double breach (main wall + Vault wall)** — the primary attacker execute for this site. Hound should reinforce the main wall first and listen for Thatcher EMPs during setup phase. If EMPs are heard late in the round, Smigs should pre-position to deny the Vault wall approach.
- **Backside push through basement** — a solo attacker will often push through the basement/backside approach. Sarge should watch for this during his roam, or Grant should be ready to cover this angle from mid-hall.
- **90 window / Vault wall attacker** — the Vault wall into Games/Pool from the 90 window is a standard attack route. Reinforce that wall or Smigs should pressure toward that angle early.
- **Red stairs roamer denial** — attackers may send a player to deny Sarge's roam from red stairs. Sarge should have drone support before committing to the roam.
- **Nomad (Airjabs)** — S-tier, ~40% ban rate. If Nomad is not banned, expect Airjabs placed on the basement/backside approach and the Gallery stairwell. An Airjab on the backside completely shuts down Sarge's disengage path. Call it immediately if Sarge triggers one — the team needs to know his route is cut.
- **Zero (Argus Cams)** — S-tier. Argus Cams fired into the Games/Pool or Aviator walls give attackers persistent intel on anchor positions and the plant spot, even after Zero dies. Peej's Valkyrie cameras are the counter — check for green Argus Cams in the walls and destroy them. A Zero cam in the Games/Pool bar area sees the entire plant zone.
- **Brava (Kludge Drone)** — A-tier. Brava's drone can hack and reverse Smigs' Aruni gate, turning it into a friendly-fire hazard against the defense. If Smigs hears the gate turn against him, he must destroy it immediately before it becomes a liability.

---

## Notes

The team wins Aviator/Games/Pool at extremely high rates. This setup mirrors what's been working naturally — the key is Kapkan coverage on Gallery/Study approaches before attackers hit site. Sarge's Vigil roam is the variable — he should hit early and disengage before the team loses a numbers advantage. Do not over-complicate this site.

strats.siteContext: 60% defense WR (355 rounds on siege.gg) — defender-sided. The site is in the back-right corner of 1F (Kitchen 4A + Dining Room 4B, connected via China). Primary attacker threat: Laundry→Dining Room breach from the east side + vertical pressure from 2F above (Astronomy above Kitchen, Statue/Connector area above Dining). The setup is built to make the Laundry approach as costly as possible and use Aruni + Kapkan to win attrition before site is ever breached.
strats.content:
# Villa — Kitchen / Dining Room (1F) · DEFENSE

**Status:** ✅ Fully Developed
**Developed:** 2026-03-06 (map-verified rewrite 2026-03-09)
**Data Source:** Team stats + Villa_1F.png + Villa_2F.png
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej (Maestro/Valkyrie) replaces Slug (Smoke/Valkyrie). Post-plant denial shifts from Smoke canisters to Maestro Evil Eye + LMG. Villa is currently ❌ Avoid in Y11S1 — contingency use only.

> **Site Context:** 60% defense WR (355 rounds on siege.gg) — defender-sided. The site is in the back-right corner of 1F (Kitchen 4A + Dining Room 4B, connected via China). Primary attacker threat: Laundry→Dining Room breach from the east side + vertical pressure from 2F above (Astronomy above Kitchen, Statue/Connector area above Dining). The setup is built to make the Laundry approach as costly as possible and use Aruni + Kapkan to win attrition before site is ever breached.

---

## Anchor Assignments

| Player | Operator | Position | Role |
|--------|----------|----------|------|
| **Hound** | Castle (or Frost / Azami) | Kitchen | Primary anchor; barricades north Kitchen window (Back Stairs side) + Dining entry through China |
| **Peej** | Maestro or Valkyrie | Dining Room | Holds Dining from rear corner; cams on all approach paths + post-plant denial |
| **Smigs** | Aruni | Laundry→Dining Room doorframe | Gate on the primary attacker breach entry — Laundry side |
| **Grant** | Kapkan or Thorn (or Mute) | Laundry / Dining approach | Trap cluster in Laundry, Dining, and Memorial approach; last-line flank guard |
| **Sarge** | Vigil | Roamer (Dining Room / Skylight Hall / Laundry area) | Early pick; disengage to Back Hall when attackers commit |

**Hound (Castle / Frost / Azami)** — Kitchen. Barricades the north-facing exterior window (Back Stairs side — the only exterior window attackers can rappel through into Kitchen) and the China entry from Dining. Holds the Kitchen interior from cover. If **Frost**: beartraps under the north window and inside the China doorframe — anyone coming through takes a trap. If **Azami**: Kiba Barriers on the north window and China entry — harder to destroy than barricades and resilient against Sledge-heavy comps where Castle panels are cleared instantly. Azami is Hound's strongest K/D DEF op in Y11S1 (7r / 71.4% / 2.33).

**Peej (Maestro / Valkyrie)** — Dining Room. Holds from the rear corner of Dining Room (furthest from Laundry breach point). Valkyrie cams: one at Laundry exterior access, one at Memorial approach, one at Skylight Hall. Maestro Evil Eye: placed on the ceiling or wall above the Laundry breach opening to provide LMG denial of the defuse attempt and persistent intel. ⚠️ Roster change 2026-03-21: Peej (Maestro/Valkyrie) replaces Slug (Smoke/Valkyrie). Post-plant denial transitions from Smoke canisters to Evil Eye + LMG. Peej must remain near his Evil Eye to operate it.

**Smigs (Aruni)** — Laundry→Dining Room doorframe. **This is the most important gadget placement on this site.** The Aruni gate goes on the Laundry→Dining Room doorframe — directly in the path of the primary attacker breach approach. Attackers must destroy the gate (grenade or sustained fire) before Smigs can place the Thermite charge cleanly. Smigs then holds Dining Room interior with Peej.

**Grant (Kapkan / Thorn / Mute)** — Laundry, Dining approach, and Memorial area. Trap cluster in: Laundry entry (the exterior access point), Dining Room entry arches from the Skylight Hall side, Memorial approach to Dining Room. If **Mute**: Signal Disruptors at Laundry drone entry points and adjacent to the Laundry→Dining Room wall — prevents attacker drones from scouting the Aruni gate and delays Thermite charge placement. Mute is Grant's actual #1 DEF op in Y11S1 (17r / 58.8%). Pick Mute when denying early attacker drone/intel on the Laundry approach is the priority; pick Kapkan when early kill pressure is the priority.

**Sarge (Vigil)** — Roamer. Starts in Dining Room or Skylight Hall. Classic Vigil play: early intel and one pick on the attacker advance (likely coming from Laundry or Back Hall). Disengage through Skylight Hall → Back Hall before attackers commit to the Laundry breach. Do NOT hold Laundry or the Aruni gate line — that's Smigs' territory. Sarge's job is an early pick and clean disengage, not a Laundry-side hold.

---

## Gadget Deployment

- **Aruni (Smigs):** Laundry→Dining Room doorframe — placed to intercept the primary attacker entry; mandatory first placement every round
- **Kapkan (Grant):** Laundry entry (exterior access), Dining Room entry arches (Skylight Hall side), Memorial approach arch
- **Mute alt (Grant):** Signal Disruptors at Laundry drone entry and adjacent to Laundry→Dining wall — use when denying attacker drone intel on the breach point is the priority
- **Maestro (Peej):** Evil Eye camera on ceiling/wall above the Laundry breach opening — LMG denial + persistent intel; placed during setup, operated during post-plant
- **Valkyrie cams (Peej):** Laundry exterior, Memorial, Skylight Hall — provides early warning on all three approach vectors to Dining Room
- **Frost beartraps (Hound alt):** Under north Kitchen window (Back Stairs side), inside China doorframe — traps the two points attackers push through to enter Kitchen from the non-Laundry side
- **Azami alt (Hound):** Kiba Barriers on north Kitchen window and China entry — use vs. Sledge-heavy comps; much harder to destroy than Castle panels

---

## Reinforcement Priority

1. **Kitchen north wall (Back Stairs side)** — seals exterior attacker access into Kitchen from Back Stairs exterior; highest priority
2. **Dining Room → Skylight Hall wall** — limits the Skylight Hall push into Dining Room from the west
3. **China walls** — reinforcing China walls tightens the internal corridor and limits angles between site rooms
4. **Memorial → Dining Room wall** — closes the secondary south approach

> **Note on Laundry→Dining Room wall:** Defenders should NOT reinforce this wall. The Aruni gate (Smigs) goes on the doorframe there — if the wall is reinforced, it removes attacker incentive to place a Thermite charge, but it also removes the Aruni gate opportunity. Leaving this wall soft forces attackers to either: (a) place Thermite and deal with the Aruni gate, or (b) push through the doorframe and take the Aruni damage. The trap field (Kapkan) in Laundry compounds this — attackers can't rush the entry.

---

## Post-Plant Hold

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Peej (Maestro)** | Dining Room rear | Evil Eye denial via LMG through Laundry doorframe or China arch — denies defuse from either entry |
| **Hound** | Kitchen depth | Final hold; hard angle from Kitchen interior; last defuser challenge |
| **Smigs** | Dining Room / China junction | Holds the connector between the two site rooms; defuser challenge position |

---

## Win Conditions

### Win A — Primary: Laundry Attrition + Site Anchor ✅
**When:** Kapkan traps hit in Laundry, Aruni gate forces a delay on the doorframe, Sarge gets one early pick from Dining, and the 4 anchors hold site.

Winning the Laundry approach means attackers enter the breach already hurt, delayed, and potentially down a player. The Aruni gate adds another layer — attackers must spend resources destroying it. If the Laundry lane costs 1–2 kills and resources, the team plays a numbers advantage into the endgame. Peej's Maestro Evil Eye wins the post-plant denial.

### Win B — Laundry Fully Neutralized (Traps and Gate Cleared) ⚠️
**When:** Attackers use drones + grenades to clear Laundry traps and destroy the Aruni gate without taking damage.

**Adjustment:** This is now a pure gunfight at the Dining Room breach. Smigs holds the China connector hard. Peej repositions to watch the Memorial approach — attackers may try to come from that side instead. Grant pulls from Laundry back to Dining depth — no more trap kills available, play for angles.

### Win C — Vertical Contested (Attackers Get 2F Above Site) ⚠️
**When:** Attacker vertical pair (Buck/Sledge) establishes hatch control in Astronomy or Connector area above Kitchen/Dining.

**Adjustment:** Peej calls all hatch drop positions from Valkyrie cams — this is Peej's primary job if vertical is happening. Maestro Evil Eye can also provide LMG suppression on vertical entry angles if placed correctly. Hound tight-anchors in Kitchen below the hatch — do not stand in open floor areas. Smigs should move out of the Dining Room ceiling exposure zone and hold a wall-adjacent angle. If vertical is fully live and uncontested, consider sending Sarge upstairs to contest the 2F position — he's already a roamer and this is a legitimate mid-round read.

---

## Watch For

- **Laundry→Dining Room breach** — the primary attacker approach on this site. Smigs' Aruni gate on that doorframe is the first line of defense. If Sarge spots the two-man Laundry crew advancing, call it early so Smigs can pre-position behind the gate and Peej can camera-watch the approach.

- **Vertical from 2F (Astronomy / Connector/Statue area above Kitchen/Dining)** — Buck and Sledge are the primary operators for vertical play. Two attackers above this site with floor control is extremely dangerous — Peej's Valkyrie cams should include an angle watching the 2F Astronomy / Back Stairs access. If vertical is spotted, Sarge should consider contesting from the Back Stairs area before the floor breach happens.

- **Back Hall / Back Stairs approach to Kitchen** — a secondary attacker route. An attacker (likely Grant as IGL) will approach from the Back Hall side to close rotation. Sarge's Vigil disengage should go through Skylight Hall, not Back Hall — they'll collide if Sarge goes the wrong way. Sarge disengage route: Dining Room → Skylight Hall → hold.

- **Memorial approach to Dining** — attackers sometimes use this as a secondary entry into Dining Room from below. Grant's Kapkan trap in Memorial / Dining Room arch covers this. Peej's Valkyrie cam on Memorial gives early warning.

- **China hold** — once attackers breach Laundry→Dining, the next push is through China into Kitchen. This is the critical chokepoint inside site. Smigs should hold China hard and force an attacker trade in the corridor.

- **Nomad (Airjabs)** — S-tier, ~40% ban rate. If Nomad is not banned, expect Airjabs at the Laundry exterior entry and along the Back Hall approach. An Airjab inside Laundry completely resets the Laundry approach — anyone moving through Laundry gets pushed back. Call Airjab positions immediately so Grant can place Kapkan traps on the correct side and Sarge adjusts his roam path.

- **Sledge (soft breach)** — A-tier, nearly universal. Sledge can destroy China soft walls to create new entry angles into Kitchen that bypass the Laundry→Dining breach entirely. Hound should reinforce China walls during setup to deny this.

- **Zero (Argus Cams)** — S-tier. Argus Cams fired into Dining Room or Kitchen walls give attackers persistent intel on anchor positions. Peej should scan walls from the Dining rear position and call any green cam indicators.

- **Brava (Kludge Drone)** — A-tier. Brava's drone can hack Smigs' Aruni gate on the Laundry→Dining doorframe. If the gate is hacked, it turns against Smigs — he must destroy it immediately. Reinforce the Laundry-adjacent walls during setup to reduce Brava drone access paths.

---

## Notes

The Aruni gate on the Laundry→Dining doorframe is the setup's defining placement — it directly counters the primary attacker breach approach. Paired with Kapkan traps in Laundry and the Kapkan/Mute choice based on attacker comp, the team makes the Laundry lane extremely expensive before anyone even reaches site. Hound anchoring the Kitchen north window denies the secondary exterior approach. Sarge's Vigil roam in Dining / Skylight Hall is the pressure valve — one early pick from the roam and the team plays a numbers advantage. Sarge's disengage route is Skylight Hall, not Back Hall.

strats.siteContext: 43% defense WR (58 rounds on siege.gg) — the most attacker-friendly site on Villa and the "shunned" site defenders avoid. Very rarely picked. Most vertical-pressure-vulnerable site on Villa. The entire setup is built around denying vertical approach. If opponents pick this, it's a gift — they're voluntarily taking the attacker-favored site.
strats.content:
# Villa — Living Room / Library (1F) · DEFENSE

**Status:** ✅ Fully Developed
**Developed:** 2026-03-06 (migrated from STACK_04 2026-03-08)
**Note:** Site names corrected from old KB ("Wine Cellar / Basement" was wrong). Correct names: Living Room / Library.
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej (Maestro/Valkyrie) replaces Slug (Smoke/Valkyrie). Post-plant denial shifts from Smoke canisters to Maestro Evil Eye + LMG. Villa is currently ❌ Avoid in Y11S1 — contingency use only.

> **Site Context:** 43% defense WR (58 rounds on siege.gg) — the most attacker-friendly site on Villa and the "shunned" site defenders avoid. Very rarely picked. Most vertical-pressure-vulnerable site on Villa. The entire setup is built around denying vertical approach. If opponents pick this, it's a gift — they're voluntarily taking the attacker-favored site.

---

## Anchor Assignments

| Player | Operator | Position | Role |
|--------|----------|----------|------|
| **Hound** | Castle or Frost (or Azami) | Living Room | Primary anchor; barricades main stair entry; holds tight angle |
| **Peej** | Maestro or Valkyrie | Library | General area hold; cams on hatches + corridor approach |
| **Smigs** | Aruni | Corridor entry | Gate on the Corridor entry — controls main stairs/circulation |
| **Grant** | Kapkan or Thorn (or Mute) | Stairwell / hatch landing zones | Traps at every hatch landing zone — highest value Kapkan spot on map |
| **Sarge** | Vigil | Upper-floor roamer (1F / stairwell area) | Does NOT go to site — stays on upper floor to deny vertical setup |

**Hound (Castle / Frost)** — Living Room. Barricades the main stair entry into Living Room. Holds the tight angle from cover. If Frost: beartraps at the bottom of every hatch landing zone — the beartraps at hatch landings are extremely punishing on this site. If Azami: Kiba Barriers on the stair entry and the hatch edges from below — use when attackers are expected to have heavy Sledge making Castle panels irrelevant. Azami barriers on hatch edges from the inside limit the angle attackers get when they open the hatch from above. Hound's Y11S1 Azami data: 7r / 71.4% / 2.33 K/D.

**Peej (Maestro / Valkyrie)** — Library area. Holds from cover. Maestro Evil Eye placed above the Living Room plant spot — LMG denial on the defuse attempt. Valkyrie cams on hatches (looking from above) and the Corridor approach. ⚠️ Roster change 2026-03-21: Peej (Maestro/Valkyrie) replaces Slug (Smoke/Valkyrie). Post-plant denial transitions from Smoke canisters to Evil Eye + LMG. Peej must remain near his Evil Eye to operate it.

**Smigs (Aruni)** — Corridor entry. Aruni gate on the Corridor entry arch — controls anyone coming through the main stairwell or Corridor circulation route.

**Grant (Kapkan / Thorn)** — Stairwell area. Kapkan traps on every hatch landing zone (multiple hatches above this site). **This is Kapkan's highest value setup in the entire map rotation** — stacking traps at hatch landings is essential here. Also trap the top of the main stairs. If Mute: on this site specifically, Kapkan is almost always the stronger choice — the hatch landing traps are too high-value to give up. Mute is worth considering only if the team reads a heavy drone-first vertical setup where attackers will clear all hatch traps before committing. Grant's Y11S1 DEF identity note: Mute is his actual #1 DEF op by rounds (17r / 58.8%) — but Living/Library is the one site where Kapkan's situational value may outweigh it.

**Sarge (Vigil)** — Upper-floor roamer. **Sarge does NOT come down to site** — he stays on the upper floor and roams the stairwell area to deny vertical pressure setup. His role is to waste attacker time on the floors above, not to anchor below. Disengage completely before attackers breach site.

---

## Gadget Deployment

- **Kapkan (Grant):** Every hatch landing zone (multiple hatches above this site) — priority. Also the top of the main stairs.
- **Mute alt (Grant):** Signal Disruptors at drone entry points and stairwell — consider only if facing a heavy drone-clearing vertical comp; Kapkan is almost always the stronger pick on this site
- **Aruni (Smigs):** Corridor entry arch
- **Frost beartraps (Hound alt):** Living Room entry, stair bottom — supplements Kapkan at choke points
- **Azami alt (Hound):** Kiba Barriers on stair entry and hatch edges from below — limits attacker angles when hatches open; use vs. Sledge-heavy comps
- **Maestro (Peej):** Evil Eye above the Living Room plant spot — LMG denial during post-plant; place during setup phase
- **Valkyrie cams (Peej):** Hatches (looking from above), Corridor approach, exterior/garden area

---

## Reinforcement Priority

1. Main stair wall — primary defender entry control into Living Room
2. Living Room ↔ Library connecting wall — splits site access
3. Corridor wall — blocks soft breach from adjacent area
4. Utility room walls (if applicable)

---

## Post-Plant Hold

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Peej (Maestro)** | Library depth | Post-plant denial via Evil Eye above plant spot; LMG on defuse attempt |
| **Hound** | Living Room anchor | Final hold position; tight angle cover |
| **Grant** | Stairwell remnant / Library depth | Watches stairwell approach post-plant |

---

## Win Conditions

### Win A — Primary: Vertical Denial + Hatch Trap Stack
**When:** Sarge successfully wastes attacker time on upper floor during setup, Grant's hatch traps hit before vertical control is established.

This site is won by denying vertical control in the first 90 seconds. If attackers can't get above site cleanly, they have to walk down the main stairs into a trap field. That's an auto-advantage.

### Win B — Vertical Control Lost (Attackers Get Hatches)
**When:** Attackers establish vertical control before Sarge can disrupt them, or Sarge dies too early.

**Adjustment:** Pull back immediately. Do not fight for hatches — that's a losing position once attackers own them. Hound reinforces the Living Room and plays the static hold. Peej watches the hatch drops from Valkyrie cams and calls where attackers drop. Smigs' Aruni gate becomes a critical delay tool.

### Win C — Stair Rush (No Setup Time)
**When:** Attackers fast-rush the main stairs without clearing the building, accepting early losses to get site control.

**Adjustment:** Grant's stair traps are the primary counter. If the rush hits a trap(s), the team pushes the numbers advantage aggressively. Hound's Castle barricade on the stair entry buys critical seconds. If traps are missed/avoided, anchor deep and make them fight for every inch.

---

## Watch For

- **Vertical setup (hatches) — Buck and Sledge** — this site is most vulnerable to vertical pressure. Buck (B-tier, Skeleton Key) and Sledge (A-tier, unlimited soft hammer) are the primary operators attackers run for vertical play. Both can open hatches and floors rapidly. Sarge's upper-floor roam is specifically to waste their time and force a trade before hatches open.
- **Stairwell rush** — fast pushes down the main stairs before traps are placed. Grant should prioritize stair traps first in setup phase.
- **Hound's Frost variant is elite here** — if Frost is available over Castle, the beartraps at every hatch landing are extremely punishing. Consider Frost when opponents are known to prioritize vertical play.
- **Nomad (Airjabs)** — S-tier, ~40% ban rate. If Nomad is not banned, expect Airjabs placed at the top of the main stairs and on the approach paths Sarge uses to roam the upper floor. An Airjab at the stair top completely shuts down the stairwell rush counter — Grant's trap placement window narrows. Call any Airjab positions immediately so the team adjusts.
- **Zero (Argus Cams)** — S-tier. Argus Cams fired through hatch openings or into Living Room / Library walls give attackers persistent site intel before the ground push. Peej should watch for green cam indicators from the Library position and destroy any that appear inside site.
- **Iana (Gemini Replicator)** — A-tier, high ban rate. If Iana is not banned, expect hologram decoys sent down through the hatches or into the stairwell to probe positions before the real push. Do not commit to a fight based on sound alone on this site — the hologram is indistinguishable by audio.

---

## Notes

This is the shunned site on Villa (43% DEF WR, only 58 rounds) — defenders avoid picking it. If the opponent picks Living/Library, they're voluntarily playing the attacker-favored side. The setup is built around the one thing that makes it defensible: denying vertical control. Sarge's upper-floor roam to waste setup time + Kapkan's hatch-trap cluster is the core of this defense. If attackers can't get vertical control in the first 90 seconds, they'll have to walk into the trap field.

strats.siteContext: 51% defense WR (488 rounds on siege.gg) — the most balanced site on Villa. Most multi-directional pressure risk from exterior and Dining area. Grant's Kapkan load in Dining is the clutch factor.
strats.content:
# Villa — Trophy / Statue (2F) · DEFENSE

**Status:** ✅ Fully Developed
**Developed:** 2026-03-06 (migrated from STACK_04 2026-03-08)
**Note:** Site names re-verified against labeled map images. Correct names confirmed: Trophy / Statue.
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej (Maestro/Valkyrie) replaces Slug (Smoke/Valkyrie). Post-plant denial shifts from Smoke canisters to Maestro Evil Eye + LMG. Villa is currently ❌ Avoid in Y11S1 — contingency use only. Prior KB entry "Statue" was wrong — the map labels this room "STATUE." The even older KB entry "Statue Room" was actually correct.

> **Site Context:** 51% defense WR (488 rounds on siege.gg) — the most balanced site on Villa. Most multi-directional pressure risk from exterior and Dining area. Grant's Kapkan load in Dining is the clutch factor.

---

## Anchor Assignments

| Player | Operator | Position | Role |
|--------|----------|----------|------|
| **Hound** | Castle (or Frost / Azami) | Statue (Bar area) | Holds bar counter and windows facing Dining; barricades key doors |
| **Peej** | Maestro or Valkyrie | Trophy | Primary anchor; cameras + post-plant denial |
| **Smigs** | Aruni | Trophy main entry | Gate on the Dining→Trophy arch |
| **Grant** | Kapkan or Thorn (or Mute) | Dining Room / Garden approach | Trap cluster on approach paths; middle flex |
| **Sarge** | Vigil | Roamer (Dining Room / Living area) | Dining pressure, disengage through Living to Hall |

**Hound (Castle)** — Statue (Bar side). Holds the bar counter and windows facing Dining Room. Barricades key doors into Statue from Dining. Difficult angle from behind the bar. If Frost: beartraps under the garden-facing window and behind the Dining-entry door. If Azami: Kiba Barriers on the garden-facing windows and Dining entry — better when attackers have heavy Sledge and Castle panels won't hold. Azami is Hound's highest individual K/D DEF op in Y11S1 (7r / 71.4% / 2.33).

**Peej (Maestro / Valkyrie)** — Trophy. Primary anchor inside Trophy. Maestro Evil Eye placed above the Trophy plant spot — provides LMG denial and persistent intel simultaneously. Valkyrie cams placed to watch exterior courtyard, Dining entry, and hallway approaches. ⚠️ Roster change 2026-03-21: Peej (Maestro/Valkyrie) replaces Slug (Smoke/Valkyrie). Post-plant denial transitions from Smoke canisters to Evil Eye + LMG. Peej must remain near the Evil Eye to operate it.

**Smigs (Aruni)** — Trophy main entry from Dining. Aruni gate on the arch — anyone coming through takes the hit. Holds far wall angle in Trophy.

**Grant (Kapkan / Thorn)** — Kapkan in Dining Room and adjacent Kitchen/Garden hall approaches. Thorn watches the main Hallway approach to Statue. Acts as a middle flex — supports either room. If Mute: Signal Disruptors at the Dining Room drone entry points and near the Trophy↔Dining wall — blocks early attacker drone coverage of the site and prevents hard breach gadgets from triggering cleanly. Pick Mute when stopping pre-execute drone intel is the priority; pick Kapkan/Thorn when early kill pressure on Dining approach is the priority. Mute is Grant's actual #1 DEF op in Y11S1 (17r / 58.8%).

**Sarge (Vigil)** — Roamer. Starts in Dining Room or Living area. Classic Vigil pressure: get one or two early picks on Dining clear, disengage through Living Room to main Hall, and re-anchor toward site.

---

## Gadget Deployment

- **Kapkan (Grant):** Dining Room entry, Garden area access, Kitchen-side approach — high foot traffic zone
- **Mute alt (Grant):** Signal Disruptors at Dining drone entries and Trophy↔Dining wall — use when pre-execute drone denial is more valuable than trap kills
- **Aruni (Smigs):** Trophy/Statue main entry arch — controls the most direct route into site
- **Maestro (Peej):** Evil Eye camera placed above the Trophy plant spot — LMG denial + persistent cam during post-plant; place during setup phase
- **Valkyrie cams (Peej):** Exterior courtyard, Dining Room center, 1F Hallway junction — early warning on multi-directional push
- **Azami alt (Hound):** Kiba Barriers on garden-facing windows and Dining entry — use vs. Sledge-heavy comps where Castle panels get destroyed quickly

---

## Reinforcement Priority

1. Trophy ↔ Dining Room wall — primary breach wall; reinforce to force a harder push
2. Statue ↔ Dining Room wall — secondary; controls Dining entry into Bar area
3. Trophy ↔ Living area wall
4. Statue exterior wall (if soft — controls garden-side window breach)

---

## Post-Plant Hold

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Peej (Maestro)** | Trophy | Post-plant denial via Evil Eye above plant spot; LMG fire on defuse attempt |
| **Hound** | Statue depth | Bar hold; watches for defuser challenge from Living area angle |
| **Grant (Thorn)** | Hallway junction | Watches main approach to both rooms |

---

## Win Conditions

### Win A — Primary: Dining Trap + Site Anchor
**When:** Grant's Kapkan traps in Dining hit, Sarge gets a Dining room pick, Aruni gate delays site entry.

Getting kills before attackers reach site from the Dining approach is the primary win condition here. If Grant gets 1–2 trap kills and Sarge gets one Dining pick, the team plays from a numbers advantage.

### Win B — Sarge Commits Too Deep / Dies
**When:** Sarge takes a bad trade in Dining and dies without disengaging.

**Adjustment:** Four anchors hold site. Grant pulls back from mid-hall and tightens toward site. Peej's Valkyrie cams become primary intel. Don't send a second person to roam — the anchor stack defends well with 4.

### Win C — Dining Fully Cleared (No Trap Kills)
**When:** Attackers methodically drone and clear every approach without triggering traps.

**Adjustment:** This is now a gunfight round. Smigs' Aruni gate is the last attrition point before site entry. Peej should be on Valkyrie for this site if trap neutralization is expected — intel advantage is the substitute when Kapkan pressure fails. Hound holds the bar line hard.

---

## Watch For

- **Multi-directional exterior pressure** — this site is exposed to garden exterior, Dining, and Hallway simultaneously. Peej's Valkyrie cams are essential to track all three angles. If Valkyrie is banned, lean on Maestro's Evil Eye for intel + denial and Kapkan kill data only.
- **Garden-side approach to Statue** — exterior window breach into the Statue/Bar side. Hound's Castle barricade on the garden-facing windows is a priority action in setup.
- **Dining Room dominant push** — attackers will almost always clear Dining before site. Sarge should engage and disengage quickly; do not let him get cornered.
- **Nomad (Airjabs)** — S-tier, ~40% ban rate. If Nomad is not banned, expect Airjabs placed on the Dining Room approach and the Garden exterior. An Airjab on the Dining entry cuts off Sarge's rotation path back from his roam. Grant should listen for Nomad gadget placement sounds during setup and call Airjab locations to the team.
- **Iana (Gemini Replicator)** — A-tier, high ban rate. If Iana is not banned, expect hologram decoys probing Trophy and Statue before the real push. The hologram makes sounds and moves like a real attacker. Do not trade kills into a hologram — Peej's Valkyrie cams can help distinguish real vs. decoy by watching for abnormal movement patterns.
- **Zero (Argus Cams)** — S-tier. Argus Cams fired into the Trophy or Statue walls give attackers persistent intel post-plant. Peej should scan walls for green Argus Cam indicators and destroy any found in site.
- **Brava (Kludge Drone)** — A-tier. Brava's drone can hack Smigs' Aruni gate. If the gate is hacked, Smigs must destroy it before it becomes a liability. Hound's Castle barricade on the garden window also prevents the drone from easily reaching the gate.

---

## Notes

This 2F site has the most multi-directional pressure risk on Villa. Grant's Kapkan load in Dining is the clutch factor — getting one or two trap kills before the team even engages tilts the round significantly. Sarge should commit to Dining roam since this is where attackers must clear first, then disengage before taking a bad trade.

overviewContent:
# Villa — Overview

> ⚠️ **Note:** This document was generated before map images were available. Room names and bomb site descriptions below may contain outdated/incorrect names. For verified room names and adjacencies, use **VILLA_MAP_REFERENCE.md** as the authoritative source. Bomb site and callout corrections are noted inline below.

**Environment:** Italian country villa; classic competitive estate with courtyard and formal gardens
**Floors:** Basement / Wine Cellar (B), Ground Floor (1F), Upper Floor (2F)
**In Y10S4 Ranked Pool:** Yes
**In Y11S1 Ranked Pool:** ✅ Yes — first half of season only. Removed at Y11S1 mid-season swap (replaced by Favela + Stadium Bravo).
**Year Added / Reworked:** Added Y3S4 (2019); reworked Y6S3 for balance; refinements in Y9+; modernization rework in Y11S1 (lighting, geometry, and texture updates per Operation Silent Hunt patch notes)

> 📌 **Y11S1 Note:** Villa has been removed from the pool at mid-season swap (already occurred). Additionally, the stack's Y11S1 data has reversed dramatically — Villa is now ❌ Avoid (Grant 33.3%, Sarge 16.7%, Hound 42.9%). The all-time numbers below are historical only. All strats in this folder are contingency-use.
> ⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej replaces Slug in the main stack. All strat files updated. See individual strat files for mechanic notes (DEF: Smoke→Maestro; ATK Aviator: Hibana reassignment required).

---

## Overview

Villa was the stack's strongest-performing map in Y10S4 (Grant 75%, Sarge 100%, Hound 85.7%, Smigs 100%, Slug 70%). **Y11S1 data reversed this completely** — all players are below 50% in Y11S1, making Villa the team's ❌ Avoid map. Villa has also exited the ranked pool at mid-season swap. This folder remains as a contingency reference in case Villa returns in a future season. The Italian villa setting features a courtyard, formal gardens, and interior spaces with good rotation flexibility. Multiple floors create vertical gameplay opportunities with good hatch access. The map rewards coordinated team play and disciplined positioning.

---

## Bomb Sites

> ⚠️ **Original site names below were WRONG. Corrected verified names (from map images) are shown.**

### Site 1 — Planes/Aviator / Games/Pool — 2F
**Rooms:** Planes/Aviator and Games/Pool (bar area with pool table)
**Description:** Most played site on Villa (655 rounds). 60% defense WR — defender-sided. 2F west wing. Primary attacker breach: Classical Hall/West Hall → Games/Pool wall (the "main wall") + Vault wall via the 90-window (Hibana role — ⚠️ operator reassignment required with current roster). 3-man clears Study before the breach. Grant backside push from basement approach.
**Common defense:** Aruni gate on connecting doorframe, Kapkan in Gallery/Study approaches, Vigil roamer from balcony/North Hall. Team wins this site at very high rates.
**Attack approach:** See ATK_Aviator_Games_Room.md.

### Site 2 — Trophy / Statue — 2F
**Rooms:** Trophy and Statue (2F north wing — above Kitchen/Dining)
**Description:** 51% defense WR (488 rounds) — most balanced site on Villa. Master Bedroom wall breach is the primary execute. Bedroom wall is almost always reinforced.
**Common defense:** Aruni on Trophy/Dining arch, Kapkan in Dining approach, Valkyrie cams, Vigil roamer in Dining/Living.
**Attack approach:** See ATK_Trophy_Statuary.md.

### Site 3 — Living Room / Library — 1F
**Rooms:** Living Room and Library (1F west/south wing)
**Description:** 43% defense WR (58 rounds) — most attacker-favored site on Villa. Rarely picked by defenders. Vertical hatch pressure from 2F above is the win condition. Extremely limited team data.
**Common defense:** See DEF_Living_Library.md.
**Attack approach:** See ATK_Living_Library.md.

### Site 4 — Kitchen / Dining — 1F
**Rooms:** Kitchen and Dining (1F north/right wing — connected internally via China)
**Description:** 60% defense WR (355 rounds) — defender-sided. Primary attacker breach: Laundry → Dining wall (east side) + vertical from 2F Astronomy/Statue-Connector area. Almost always appears as the third site option (third attack).
**Common defense:** See DEF_Kitchen_Dining.md.
**Attack approach:** See ATK_Kitchen_Dining.md.

---
